# AUDIT_REPORT — Full-History Logic Audit

**Repository:** AI Job Hunter (Tauri + pnpm monorepo)
**Scope:** every commit from the initial release (2026-05-20) through `v0.123.0` (2026-07-07) — **1,341 commits, 563 pull requests, 216 release tags**, plus all project docs/ADRs/lessons and **587 owner coding-session transcripts**.
**Question answered:** _Is the codebase, as it exists today, logically coherent with every change requested over the project's lifetime — and where is it not?_
**Mode:** read-only. No source file was modified. This report is the only deliverable; triage happens separately.

---

## How to read this report

- **§1 Executive Summary** — the verdict and the top 10 issues.
- **§2 Requirements Ledger** — all 1285 reconstructed requirements, chronological, month-clustered.
- **§3 Traceability Matrix** — every requirement → implementing commits/PRs → current status → audit verdict.
- **§4 Findings by Severity** — the 141 defects that survived evidence-validation and arbitration, each with SHA + `path:line` + a fix.
- **§5 Contradiction Map** — requirement pairs that conflict over time and how the code resolves each.
- **§6 Open Questions** — where the original intent was ambiguous; answer these before any fix work.

### Coverage & confidence (the audit auditing itself)

| Gate                                             | Result                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Commits examined                                 | **1,341 / 1,341** (enumerated via `git rev-list`; plain `git log` silently under-counts this repo by 122) |
| PRs examined                                     | **563 / 563** (490 merged, 73 closed-unmerged)                                                            |
| Owner transcript sessions mined                  | **587** (2,652 human request-turns)                                                                       |
| Requirements reconstructed                       | **1285** (after merging 132 cross-source duplicates + linking 42 supersede chains)                        |
| Findings evidence-checked                        | **100%** — every SHA ∈ history, every `path:line` ∈ HEAD (mechanical)                                     |
| High-severity findings adversarially re-verified | **5 / 5** — **0 refuted**                                                                                 |
| Medium/low spot-checked                          | **27 (20% sample)** — **0 citations failed**                                                              |

### Requirement status distribution (1285 live entries)

| Status                              | Count | Share |
| ----------------------------------- | ----- | ----- |
| Implemented                         | 875   | 68%   |
| Documented (standing rule/decision) | 308   | 24%   |
| Superseded                          | 40    | 3%    |
| Partial                             | 19    | 1%    |
| Abandoned (closed-unmerged)         | 17    | 1%    |
| Reverted                            | 5     | 0%    |
| Unclear (→ §6)                      | 21    | 2%    |

### Findings by severity

**0 critical · 5 high · 61 medium · 75 low** (141 total; 4 dropped in arbitration).

### Method (how the ledger and findings were produced)

1. **Reconstruct intent** — 71 miners extracted candidate requirements from commit chunks, PR bodies, transcript bundles, and every doc/ADR/lesson/agent-artifact; 20 correlators bound owner asks to the commits/PRs that implemented them (timestamp proximity, PR-number and branch-name links, revert/supersede chains); a stitcher merged cross-slice duplicates.
2. **Audit against current code** — 24 domain auditors + 34 cross-cutting agents (timeline-contradiction per topic, 9 boundary-seam audits, 4 verification mappers) checked HEAD against the ledger across five lenses: traceability, logic consistency, drift, orphans/ghosts, regression.
3. **Verify** — the full check suite (typecheck, lint, tests, Rust fmt/check/clippy, drift checks, build) was run; results in the appendix. Every finding's evidence was mechanically validated; every high was adversarially re-verified by an independent agent; disputes were resolved by re-reading the disputed code.

> **Verification caveat:** `cargo test` could not launch locally (Windows loader error `STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND`, an environment issue, not a code defect); Rust test coverage was corroborated against CI. All other checks passed. See appendix.

---

### 1. Executive Summary

The codebase at HEAD (v0.123.0) is largely coherent with what you asked for across the project's life. Of the 1,417 requirement-status entries the audit reconstructed from 1,341 commits, all 563 PRs, the docs/ADRs/lessons, and 587 session transcripts (covering ~1,285 distinct requirements), 1,309 — about **92%** — sit at _implemented_ or _documented_, and the intentional-lifecycle outcomes (40 superseded, 17 abandoned, 6 reverted) reflect a later ask overriding an earlier one, not drift. Auditing the current code against every requirement produced 141 findings and — the headline — **zero critical and only five high-severity findings**, and those five collapse to **three distinct real defects**. Just 16 of the ~1,285 requirement IDs are implicated in any high finding. The project does what you asked of it; the incoherences are few and concentrated, not systemic.

Where it is _not_ coherent clusters in four areas. **(1) i18n completeness** — the strongest correctness signal: three independent auditors (`i18n-001`, `p2-m-i18n-001`, `p2-b5-translations-seam-001`) each landed on the _same_ five `t()` keys that live, mounted components reference but that exist in neither `en` nor `de`, so users see raw key paths on real screens (a settings toast, the onboarding browser-error state, a cloud-provider Cancel button) — the exact HIGH-blocking class you banned after the `jobs.boards.glassdoor` raw-literal incident. **(2) The dead / unreachable Support-diagnostics feature** — the strongest _theme_ in the whole audit, hit by five separate auditors: the entire support diagnostics / health / recovery / knowledge-base UI subtree (~22 components), plus the reviewed and translated Export-Diagnostics "submit crash logs" action (REQ-14020) and the Settings→Support copy buttons (REQ-00040), have no route and no importer and have been unreachable since the initial release; ~45 orphaned `t()` keys hang off this dead subtree and are the main thing jamming the i18n drift check. **(3) Documentation drift** — the single largest finding class (84 of 141 findings are drift/consistency/contradiction): board counts (README says 16, the registry has 23), bundled fonts (docs promise Noto Sans; the engine ships Carlito/Inter/Source Serif/Manrope and no Noto), the storage layer (docs still describe the deleted `app.db` + LanceDB, code is per-domain SQLite), the accent color (docs say violet, the app ships teal), and the release model (a skill file still says "on push to main" when releases are manual-dispatch only). **(4) Privacy-claim accuracy** — SECURITY.md/README assert the only outbound calls are the AI provider + web search, contradicted by location geocoding (Nominatim), job-board scraping (Adzuna/LinkedIn/ATS), the on-launch updater (GitHub), and opt-in Clearbit egress.

Confidence in these findings is high. Every finding was mechanically evidence-checked (SHA present in history, path+line present at HEAD), and every high-severity finding was adversarially re-verified: **0 findings were refuted on re-verification, and 0 citations failed spot-check.** All five highs carry an arbiter verdict of _confirmed_ at _high_ confidence. The contradiction pass adds texture — 271 requirement-pair conflicts, of which 197 are cleanly resolved (the later ask wins and the code follows) and 74 are problematic, but those are overwhelmingly a stale doc or an un-superseded older ledger entry rather than broken behavior. Net verdict: sound on behavior, with a short, concrete punch-list dominated by five missing i18n keys, one genuinely dead user-facing feature, and documentation that has drifted behind the code.

#### Top 10 Issues

| Rank | Severity | Finding                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Where                                                                                                                                                        | Why it matters                                                                                                                                                         |
| ---- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1    | HIGH     | `i18n-001` — five live `t()` call sites reference keys absent from **both** `en` and `de`; no `defaultValue` and no missing-key handler, so i18next renders the raw dotted key                                       | `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/preferences/ResumePreferences/index.tsx:73` (also `BrowserErrorState:34`, `CloudProviderConfig:109`) | User-visible broken text on real screens (settings toast, onboarding error state, Cancel button) — the exact HIGH-blocking class you hardened a review gate to prevent |
| 2    | HIGH     | `p2-m-i18n-001` — the _same_ five missing keys, found independently from the extractor side (`i18n:extract` adds them, 0 removed)                                                                                    | `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:2468`                                                                                                 | Independent confirmation of #1 from the locale-file side; the advisory-only CI check cannot block it                                                                   |
| 3    | HIGH     | `p2-b5-translations-seam-001` — the _same_ five keys, found independently across the renderer↔`@ajh/translations` seam (used→defined direction)                                                                      | `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/onboarding/steps/BrowserStep/index.tsx:50`                                                                               | Third independent hit on the identical defect — the strongest single correctness signal in the audit                                                                   |
| 4    | HIGH     | `p2-contra-cross-001` — the "only outbound calls are the AI provider + web search" privacy guarantee is materially false                                                                                             | `README.md:93` (and `SECURITY.md:50`; egress at `scraping/boards/aggregator/mod.rs:283`, updater `lib.rs:724`)                                               | A privacy-conscious user is misled about the real network surface: location→OpenStreetMap, scraping→Adzuna/LinkedIn/ATS, launch→GitHub                                 |
| 5    | HIGH     | `renderer-feat-3-001` — the Export-Diagnostics action (REQ-14020) and Support copy buttons (REQ-00040) live in `ContactFeedback`, which has zero importers and is on no route                                        | `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/ContactFeedback/index.tsx:26` (`/support` renders FAQ-only `SupportPage`)                             | A shipped, reviewed, translated "submit crash logs after a crash" feature is unreachable by any user                                                                   |
| 6    | MED      | `rust-data-001` — factory reset never deletes the persisted Chromium login profiles under `browser-state/<board_id>/`                                                                                                | `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/board_login/mod.rs:152`                                                                                                 | Data-integrity/privacy: a "fresh-install wipe" leaves authenticated board sessions on disk                                                                             |
| 7    | MED      | `export-b-001` — the cover-letter DOCX backend hardcodes A4 (`LocaleProfile::default`) and ignores the request locale                                                                                                | `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/pdf/mod.rs:168`                                                                                                           | A US-market cover letter exports as US Letter in PDF but A4 in DOCX — breaks the "PDF and DOCX must agree on page size" contract                                       |
| 8    | MED      | `p2-b7-events-deeplink-002` — Rust emits `scrape:progress`/`scrape:item` but no renderer listener subscribes; for autopilot scraping this is the _only_ delivery channel                                             | `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/autopilot_helpers/mod.rs:69`                                                                                                     | Autopilot scraping shows no live progress and silently drops streamed items — a broken user-facing flow                                                                |
| 9    | MED      | `p2-contra-renderer-features-002` — keyword-only is the documented default (REQ-16011), but Rust `semantic_enabled_bit(None)` returns semantic-**ON**, enshrined by a passing test; the agent match tool inherits it | `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/match_resume.rs:44`                                                                                                     | The core matching feature diverges from the asked-for default — agent-driven matches silently run embeddings (extra cost + different scores)                           |
| 10   | MED      | `scraping-b-002` — the explicitly requested 15-minute posted-date filter was never implemented; finest preset is 30m in both the TS schema and the Rust mirror                                                       | `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/date_filters.rs:5`                                                                                                 | A specific, named user ask that never shipped in any commit                                                                                                            |

---

### 4. Findings by Severity

All 141 findings that survived mechanical evidence validation (SHA ∈ history, path+line ∈ HEAD) and arbitration. Every high-severity finding was adversarially re-verified by an independent agent; a 20% sample of medium/low was spot-checked (0 citations failed).

#### 🟠 High (5)

##### `i18n-001` · i18n · _traceability_ — _arbiter: confirmed_

**Five live, rendered t() call sites reference i18n keys absent from BOTH en and de, so users see the raw key string — exactly the HIGH-blocking defect class the owner banned.**

REQ-16064 / REQ-16411 make a t() that references a key missing from en or de a HIGH blocking defect. The owner hardened this into the frontend-reviewer i18n-completeness gate (PR #459 / 4b6ecd4d) specifically after 'jobs.boards.glassdoor' shipped as a raw literal (PR #457 / 11ab8c0a). Current HEAD reintroduces that exact class: five rendered call sites pass no defaultValue and reference keys absent from BOTH locales, so i18next (fallbackLng:'en', no parseMissingKeyHandler) returns the raw key to the user. (1) ResumePreferences success/error toasts — settings.resume.defaultSet (line 71) and settings.resume.defaultSetFailed (line 73); the sibling keys setDefault/setAsDefault DO exist, so this is a near-miss missed on a branch. (2) The onboarding browser-check error state (BrowserErrorState, rendered by BrowserStep line 50) — onboarding.browser.checkFailed (line 32) and checkFailedDesc (line 34); ~18 sibling onboarding.browser.* keys exist but these two do not. (3) The cloud-provider key-change Cancel button — settings.cancel (CloudProviderConfig line 109). All five confirmed null in en and de via the locale JSON. en/de parity is otherwise perfect (2178 identical keys), and the extractor prunes nothing — so these are genuine gaps, not noise. They slipped because the i18n:extract 'missing key' CI check is advisory-only by design (REQ-06050), so nothing blocks; enforcement relies on the human/agent review gate, which missed them. (The same repo proves the risk is understood: trust-badge.tsx's dynamic jobs.trust.flags.${flag} lookup is fully key-covered and guarded by a dedicated test.)

- **Evidence:** `packages/translations/src/index.ts:56` @ `HEAD` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/preferences/ResumePreferences/index.tsx:73` @ `HEAD` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/onboarding/steps/browser/BrowserErrorState/index.tsx:34` @ `HEAD`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16411, REQ-16064, REQ-11044, REQ-11042, REQ-06050
- **Fix:** Add the 5 keys (settings.resume.defaultSet, settings.resume.defaultSetFailed, settings.cancel, onboarding.browser.checkFailed, onboarding.browser.checkFailedDesc) to BOTH en and de — or repoint the call sites to existing equivalents (e.g. a shared cancel label). Given this recurs, promote the i18n:extract 'missing key' check from advisory to blocking for keys referenced by non-dead code so raw keys cannot ship.
- **Arbiter note:** All 5 t() calls verified at HEAD (ResumePreferences 71/73, BrowserErrorState 32/34, CloudProviderConfig 109), no defaultValue; all 5 keys null in en+de (src+dist). i18n index.ts:56 fallbackLng:'en', no parseMissingKeyHandler -> raw key renders. Components reachable (BrowserStep 50, ProviderRow 160). Ledger REQ-16411 makes this HIGH blocking; parity 2178 keys.

##### `p2-b5-translations-seam-001` · p2-b5-translations-seam · _boundary_ — _arbiter: confirmed_

**Five t() keys used by live, mounted renderer components are absent from both en and de locales, so users see raw i18n key strings instead of text.**

The renderer<->@ajh/translations seam is broken in the used->defined direction for keys that actually render. `onboarding.browser.checkFailed` / `checkFailedDesc` are called by BrowserErrorState (mounted in BrowserStep via `{showError && <BrowserErrorState/>}`), yet en's onboarding.browser block (which has notDetected/notDetectedDesc/downloadChrome...) has no checkFailed child — the only `checkFailed` in en is an unrelated update-check string. `settings.cancel` is a Cancel button in CloudProviderConfig (live via ProviderRow -> AISettingsTab); en's settings block has no direct `cancel` child (sibling cancels exist only under deeper sub-blocks). `settings.resume.defaultSet` / `defaultSetFailed` are toast messages in ResumePreferences (mounted in SettingsContent); en's settings.resume block has `setAsDefault`/`setDefault` but not `defaultSet`. None of these t() calls pass a defaultValue, and i18next is inited without a missing-key handler (packages/translations/src/index.ts), so a missing key renders as the raw dotted path. en and de share an identical 2178-key set, so every key is missing from BOTH locales. This is the exact bug class REQ-03040 already fixed ('contact settings showing raw i18n keys') and that REQ-09020 mandates preventing ('validate new keys by grepping their t() call sites at review time'); the components were extended without the locale side following.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/onboarding/steps/BrowserStep/index.tsx:50` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/SettingsContent/index.tsx:89` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/ai-settings/ProviderRow/index.tsx:160` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/src/index.ts:49` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-09020, REQ-03040, REQ-06018
- **Fix:** Add the 5 keys to en/de (checkFailed/checkFailedDesc under onboarding.browser, defaultSet/defaultSetFailed under settings.resume, and settings.cancel — or repoint CloudProviderConfig at the existing shared cancel key). These render on real screens (an onboarding error state, a settings toast, a settings button), so they are user-visible today.
- **Arbiter note:** All 5 keys resolve undefined in en+de (nested JSON lookup, no flat fallback). Call sites have no defaultValue: BrowserErrorState:32/34, ResumePreferences:71/73, CloudProviderConfig:109. translations/src/index.ts:49 has no missingKeyHandler so raw key renders. Live mounts: BrowserStep:50, SettingsContent:89, ProviderRow:160. Ledger REQ-03040/09020 back the bug-class story.

##### `p2-contra-cross-001` · p2-contra-cross · _contradiction_ — _arbiter: confirmed_

**The security-policy privacy guarantee 'the only outbound calls are to the AI provider + web search' is contradicted by geocoding (Nominatim), scraping aggregators/boards (Adzuna/LinkedIn/ATS), and the on-launch updater (GitHub).**

SECURITY.md:50 and README.md:93 state the app's ONLY outbound calls are to the user-configured AI provider and opt-in web search (privacy rule REQ-16455/REQ-16108). In reality: geocode.suggest fetches https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org on every location keystroke (REQ-16713), sending the user's typed location to OpenStreetMap; scrape_boards sends the search query+location to api.adzuna.com, LinkedIn, and ~12 company ATS endpoints (REQ-12010/REQ-16456 - the app's core feature); and updater.check pings github.com on every launch (REQ-16500). None of these are 'the AI provider' or 'web search', so this absolute privacy claim in the official security policy is materially false and a privacy-conscious user is misled about the actual network surface (esp. the non-obvious location->OSM disclosure).

- **Evidence:** `README.md:93` @ `HEAD` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/aggregator/mod.rs:283` @ `HEAD` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:724` @ `HEAD`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16455, REQ-16108, REQ-16713, REQ-12010, REQ-16500
- **Fix:** Qualify the claim in SECURITY.md:50 and README.md:93: the only outbound calls carrying your resume/generation CONTENT are to the AI provider you configure and opt-in web search; separately disclose that job-board scraping (Adzuna/LinkedIn/ATS boards), location autocomplete (OpenStreetMap/Nominatim), and update checks (GitHub, on launch) also make network requests. Do not assert an absolute 'only outbound calls are X'.
- **Arbiter note:** Verbatim confirmed: SECURITY.md:50 + README.md:93 assert absolute 'only outbound calls are AI provider + web search', yet geocoding.rs:78 hits nominatim (in zero .md docs), aggregator/mod.rs:283 hits api.adzuna.com, and lib.rs:724+tauri.conf.json:74 auto-ping github 10s post-launch/every 4h. Ledger REQ-16455 IS that rule; REQ-16500/16713 the updater/geocode. Not refutable.

##### `p2-m-i18n-001` · p2-m-i18n · _verification_ — _arbiter: confirmed_

**Five live, mounted UI strings reference i18n keys that are absent from BOTH en and de locale files, so users see raw key paths — the exact class REQ-16411/REQ-16064 declare HIGH-blocking.**

Running i18n:extract adds 70 keys with 0 removed; among them are genuinely-missing keys referenced by rendered components. onboarding.browser.checkFailed / checkFailedDesc are rendered by BrowserErrorState (mounted via BrowserStep) but were introduced in commit 6ac2b72f without matching locale entries; the onboarding.browser object (en line 2468, de line 2472) still lacks them. settings.cancel is rendered on the CloudProviderConfig cancel button (line 109) but the settings object (line 435) has no cancel child. settings.resume.defaultSet / defaultSetFailed are notify toasts in ResumePreferences (lines 71/73) but settings.resume (line 866) only defines setAsDefault/setDefault. All five are undefined in en AND de, so i18next returns the literal key path at runtime. This contradicts REQ-16064 ('every referenced key must exist in BOTH en and de locale files ... correct key nesting - all HIGH/blocking') and REQ-16411, and for the browser step also REQ-01052 ('localized in English and German'). No automated test guards this; the only check is the advisory CI job neutralized by Finding 002.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/onboarding/steps/browser/BrowserErrorState/index.tsx:32` @ `6ac2b72f31` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:2468` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:866` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/preferences/ResumePreferences/index.tsx:71` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/ai-settings/CloudProviderConfig/index.tsx:109` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16411, REQ-16064, REQ-01052
- **Fix:** Add the five keys to BOTH packages/translations/src/locales/{en,de}/translation.json: onboarding.browser.checkFailed + checkFailedDesc, settings.cancel, settings.resume.defaultSet + defaultSetFailed. Then wire a blocking missing-key subset check (see Finding 002) so this class cannot regress.
- **Arbiter note:** All 5 keys referenced by mounted components (BrowserErrorState idx.tsx:32/34 via steps-config.ts:15; CloudProviderConfig:109; ResumePreferences:71/73) and absent in both locales: en browser obj 2468-2487, de 2472-2491 lack checkFailed/Desc (the 2572 checkFailed is updater); settings=435-1050 has no cancel; settings.resume 866-889 has setDefault, zero defaultSet. i18n fallbackLng=en (also missing), no missing-key handler => raw key shown. REQ-16411 rules this HIGH-blocking.

##### `renderer-feat-3-001` · renderer-feat-3 · _traceability_ — _arbiter: confirmed_

**The Export Diagnostics action (REQ-14020) and the three Support copy buttons (REQ-00040) are unreachable in the running app: their host component ContactFeedback is not rendered by any route or page.**

REQ-00040 (PR #40) made the three Settings>Support copy buttons work, and REQ-14020 (PR #509, iterated through multiple review rounds) added an 'Export Diagnostics' bundle action 'on the Support/Contact page'. Both features live in features/support/components/ContactFeedback (export action wired to useExportDiagnostics -> support_export_diagnostics; copy buttons wired to copyEnvironmentDetails/copyAppVersion/copySystemInfo). But the /support route renders only SupportPage, which renders only the FAQ accordion (getSupportSections). A whole-tree grep confirms ContactFeedback has ZERO importers outside its own dir (the features/support barrel that once re-exported it has also been deleted). git show at the REQ-14020 commit (87f0b97c) shows SupportPage was already FAQ-only then and ContactFeedback had no consumer even at initial release (e2d4438). Net: the shipped, reviewed, translated Export-Diagnostics feature — whose stated purpose is 'give users a way to submit crash logs after a crash' — cannot be reached by any user, and neither can the REQ-00040 copy buttons.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/support.tsx:5` @ `HEAD` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/ContactFeedback/index.tsx:26` @ `HEAD`
- **Requirements:** REQ-14020, REQ-00040
- **Fix:** Mount ContactFeedback (or at least its Export-Diagnostics + copy-details actions) somewhere reachable — a tab on the /support page or a Settings>Support/Privacy section — so the REQ-14020 and REQ-00040 features are actually usable; alternatively, if the Support page was intentionally reduced to FAQ-only, delete ContactFeedback and reroute the export action, and update the ledger status.
- **Arbiter note:** Whole-repo grep: ContactFeedback has zero importers (only self-def index.tsx:26 + ADR). routes/support.tsx renders only SupportPage; SupportPage:10 is FAQ-only getSupportSections. ContactFeedback hosts exportDiagnostics (index.tsx:88) + 3 copy buttons (144/157/169); its 4 service hooks have no other consumer. No barrel/lazy/settings mount. Ledger: REQ-14020 & REQ-00040 both 'implemented' yet unreachable.

#### 🟡 Medium (61)

##### `ci-release-001` · ci-release · _drift_

**`.releaserc.json` maps a BREAKING CHANGE to a `minor` bump, but every release doc and the recorded decision say it must be `major` — an undocumented, unreconciled config change.**

REQ-03080 recorded the release policy as 'feat=minor, fix/perf=patch, BREAKING CHANGE=major', and the first release config (REQ-00006, commit 2f208c12) plus the post-GH013 rewrite (REQ-00030, commit 4421de7f) both set `{ "breaking": true, "release": "major" }`. Sometime before 2026-06-16 (commit 3f8227fe already shows it) the rule was silently changed to `{ "breaking": true, "release": "minor" }` (current .releaserc.json:24) with no ledger entry or doc update. Meanwhile FOUR still-current docs continue to promise major: docs/DEPLOYMENT.md:122 ('BREAKING CHANGE footer | major (x.0.0)'), CLAUDE.md:87, docs/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md:375, and .claude/skills/deployment-rules/SKILL.md:12/35. Net effect: a maintainer who adds a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer expecting v1.0.0 (as all docs state) instead gets a 0.x minor bump; the project can never auto-reach 1.0.0 via the documented mechanism. (Secondary, same class: .releaserc.json:15/16 give `ui`→patch and `revert`→patch — both cut releases and appear in notes — yet CLAUDE.md:87 lists `ui` and `revert` under the 'none' group.) minor-on-breaking is a defensible intentional choice for a 0.x line, so the config is likely correct and the docs are stale — but the divergence is unreconciled either way.

- **Evidence:** `.releaserc.json:24` @ `4421de7f70` · `.releaserc.json:15` @ `2f208c12ca` · `docs/DEPLOYMENT.md:122` @ `4421de7f70`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03080, REQ-00006, REQ-00030
- **Fix:** Reconcile config and docs: if staying in 0.x deliberately, update docs/DEPLOYMENT.md:122, CLAUDE.md:87, docs/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md:375 and deployment-rules SKILL.md to state 'BREAKING CHANGE → minor (0.x.0) while pre-1.0' and fix CLAUDE.md's ui/revert placement (they are patch, not none); OR if the documented major bump is actually wanted, restore `{ "breaking": true, "release": "major" }`. Add a one-line ADR/ledger note recording the chosen policy so it stops drifting.

##### `docs-agents-001` · docs-agents · _drift_

**Board-count claims are stale and mutually contradictory across README/docs/landing (16, 20, 21, '18+') while the SCRAPERS registry actually holds 23 boards — violating the owner's standing 'counts must come from the registry' rule.**

History: ADR-026 (commit bd3c3442, PR #469, 2026-06-21) retired 5 anti-bot scrapers, shrinking SCRAPERS 21->16, and the landing/README copy was rewritten to '16 boards'. The owner then hard-codified a rule that board counts must always be derived from the SCRAPERS registry and never copied (lessons REQ-12019, agent config REQ-16059; single-source ask REQ-12018). Afterward the registry grew back to 23 (ATS-board additions, REQ-14033 era) but the copy was never resynced. Current HEAD: SCRAPERS in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/mod.rs has 23 entries (awk-counted). Only docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md:15 ('23 boards') is correct. Everything else is stale and inconsistent: README.md:91 says '16 job boards' while README.md:513 says '21 boards' (self-contradiction inside one file); docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70 says '21 boards'; docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:32 says '18+ boards' and :439 says 'Job boards (20)'; landing/index.html (lines 8,16,556,714,775), landing/architecture-map.html (3x), landing/how-it-works.html (incl. a `/* ~16 boards */` code comment at 1524), and landing/social-card.html all say '16 boards'. The check-landing-drift.mjs guard does not validate counts (it only checks paths/IPC-contracts/registries/dead-terms/secrets, and only for architecture-map.html + how-it-works.html — not index.html), so this drift is uncaught by CI.

- **Evidence:** `README.md:91` @ `bd3c34424d` · `README.md:513` @ `bd3c34424d` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70` @ `bd3c34424d` · `landing/index.html:8` @ `bd3c34424d` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:439` @ `bd3c34424d`
- **Requirements:** REQ-12017, REQ-12019, REQ-16059, REQ-12018, REQ-05031
- **Fix:** Resync every board-count mention to the SCRAPERS registry (currently 23): README.md:91 and :513, docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:32 and :439, and all landing/*.html + social-card.html. Better: extend check-landing-drift.mjs / check-agent-system.mjs to derive the count from boards/mod.rs and fail on any hardcoded mismatch, so this class of drift is CI-caught like the owner intended.

##### `docs-agents-002` · docs-agents · _consistency_

**deployment-rules/SKILL.md still states releases are 'Automated via semantic-release on push to main', contradicting CLAUDE.md, prepare-release.md, and the actual workflow_dispatch-only release.yml (nothing runs on push).**

History: PR #418 (commit f54b3f4f, 2026-06-18) 'gate release behind a manual trigger' converted the release pipeline to manual dispatch; release.yml is now workflow_dispatch-only (header line 8 'nothing runs automatically on push to main'; job guard `if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.action == 'release'`). CLAUDE.md:83 codifies this ('Manual release ... Nothing auto-runs on push to main'), as does .claude/commands/prepare-release.md (REQ-16403: 'releases are manually dispatched via the Release workflow'). But .claude/skills/deployment-rules/SKILL.md:8 (owned by project-steward, loaded for /prepare-release and any release-file change) still opens with 'Automated via semantic-release on push to main.' The skill was even re-verified the day AFTER the manual-trigger landed (its own 'External standards ... (verified 2026-06-19)' annotation, from PR #433) yet this top-line trigger claim was left stale. The ledger pre-flagged this as REQ-16404 ('re-verify the skill file was updated') — it was not. An agent that loads deployment-rules for a release task is told the wrong trigger model.

- **Evidence:** `.claude/skills/deployment-rules/SKILL.md:8` @ `f54b3f4f3b` · `CLAUDE.md:83` @ `f54b3f4f3b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16404, REQ-16403
- **Fix:** Rewrite deployment-rules/SKILL.md:8 to match the manual-dispatch reality, e.g. 'Releases run only on manual workflow_dispatch (Actions -> Release -> action: release); semantic-release then derives the bump/notes/tag. Nothing auto-runs on push to main; never hand-tag or hand-bump.'

##### `export-a-001` · export-a · _drift_

**The normative export contract (EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md) claims the Typst engine vendors exactly two font families (Carlito + Noto Sans), but Noto Sans was never bundled — the engine has shipped Carlito + Inter + Source Serif 4 + Manrope since the migration commit.**

REQ-04031 (PR #228, the Typst migration) asked to 'vendor Carlito/Noto fonts'. The migration commit itself (60c00dea) already vendored Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope (and JetBrains Mono, later dropped) — no Noto Sans TTF ever existed in the tree. Yet EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md, which declares itself the normative contract (REQ-07023) and was last updated 2026-06-11 (after the migration), still states 'Two font families are vendored… Carlito… Noto Sans (Latin + Cyrillic subsets) | all templates (body fallback)' and repeats 'bundled Typst fonts (Carlito + Noto Sans)' in the Output-languages section; the CJK follow-up note ('bundle Noto Sans CJK') builds on the same false premise. Cyrillic coverage actually comes from Inter (world.rs comment: 'Inter — good Unicode / Cyrillic coverage'). Behavior is fine (Latin/Cyrillic render, CJK is tofu as documented), but the contract misstates what ships in every binary and would misdirect any future font/CJK work.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/world.rs:72` @ `60c00dea1b` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:294` @ `60c00dea1b` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:266` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04031, REQ-16700, REQ-07023
- **Fix:** Update EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md §Fonts and §Output languages to the real bundle (Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope; 11 faces in world.rs) and re-anchor the CJK follow-up to whichever family the project actually intends to add.

##### `export-a-002` · export-a · _drift_

**The normative contract still promises the Phase-4 auto-fix ('a two-column layout whose sections interleave when read back is re-exported single-column'), but PR #277 deliberately downgraded two-column section_order to a warning, so interleaving can no longer trigger the auto-fix — and the leftover auto-fix machinery misattributes any residual firing to interleaving.**

REQ-03020 (PR #184) shipped the auto-fix: interleaving two-column output re-exported single-column. REQ-05038 (PR #277, commit b0b919bb, 2026-06-04) then made two-column out-of-order extraction advisory ('no spurious single-column downgrade' — the in-code comment says keeping it critical 'made validate_and_fix silently re-render single-column, overriding the user's explicit two-column + ATS-off choice'). That newer owner intent is what the code does. Never reconciled: (1) EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md step 2 'Auto-fix' (line 314) and the validate/mod.rs module header (lines 8-12) still describe the old interleave-triggered behavior, although the doc was 'Last updated: 2026-06-11', a week after #277; ledger REQ-16701 still records the auto-fix as implemented behavior. (2) The can_linearize branch in validate_and_fix now only fires when a two-column export has some OTHER surviving critical (stray_markdown, header_url_mismatch) that linearizing cannot cure, yet it still re-renders in ATS mode and pushes the fixed-message 'because the two-column layout's sections interleaved when read back' — a wrong reason string attached to a layout change the defect did not cause.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/validate/mod.rs:564` @ `b0b919bb48` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/validate/mod.rs:130` @ `b0b919bb48` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:314` @ `c6f068078a` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/validate/mod.rs:8` @ `b0b919bb48`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03020, REQ-05038, REQ-16701, REQ-07023
- **Fix:** Reconcile the contract with #277: rewrite EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md §Validation step 2 and the validate/mod.rs module header to say interleaving is advisory (ATS mode is the user's control), and either scope the can_linearize auto-fix to criticals that linearizing can actually fix or make its fixed-message name the real triggering issue codes.

##### `export-b-001` · export-b · _regression_ — _arbiter: downgrade (was higher)_

**The cover-letter DOCX backend hardcodes A4 (LocaleProfile::default) and ignores the request locale, so a US-market cover letter exports as US Letter in PDF but A4 in DOCX — violating the 'PDF and DOCX must always agree on page size' contract.**

REQ-03019 (PR #183) made LocaleProfile/PageGeometry the single source of page size for BOTH PDF and DOCX, deferring per-request locale sizing to a later phase. REQ-03024 (PR #188) then explicitly completed that deferral end-to-end ('a US recommendation renders US Letter, others A4'), and EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md (the normative contract, REQ-16691) states at line 121 'PDF and DOCX ... must always agree on the page size for a given request' with US->US Letter at line 116. The resume DOCX path was wired correctly: generate_docx() passes request.page_geometry() into model_docx (docx/mod.rs:262, which resolves us->Letter via types.rs page_geometry). But the cover-letter DOCX path was left on the deferred stub: generate_cover_letter_docx() applies page_size_dxa() (docx/mod.rs:198), and page_size_dxa() returns LocaleProfile::default().page_geometry() = A4 unconditionally (docx/mod.rs:19), never reading request.locale. Its own comment (docx/mod.rs:16-17) still says 'per-request locale sizing arrives in a later phase' — a stale deferral note whose 'later phase' (REQ-03024) already shipped for resumes but not cover letters. Meanwhile the PDF cover letter DOES honor the market: letter.rs page_dims(market) returns US Letter when conv.page=='letter', and locale/letter.rs confirms 'us is the only Letter-size market' (assert conventions("us").page == "letter", line 235). Net: every US-market cover letter exported as DOCX is A4 while its PDF twin (and the US resume DOCX) is US Letter — a guaranteed, user-visible page-size mismatch across a single application's documents, contradicting an explicit 'always agree' requirement.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/pdf/mod.rs:168` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/docx/mod.rs:262` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:120` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03024, REQ-16691, REQ-03019
- **Fix:** Thread the request page geometry into the cover-letter DOCX path: give generate_cover_letter_docx the request's PageGeometry (or the resolved market) and use it in place of page_size_dxa()'s LocaleProfile::default(), so a US-market cover letter renders US Letter in DOCX exactly as the PDF backend does. Then delete the stale 'per-request locale sizing arrives in a later phase' comment and add a parity test asserting PDF and DOCX page size match for a us-locale cover-letter request.
- **Arbiter note:** Holds at HEAD: page_size_dxa() hardcodes LocaleProfile::default()=A4 (docx/mod.rs:19), used by cover-letter DOCX (mod.rs:198); resume DOCX threads request.page_geometry() (mod.rs:262) and PDF derives market=request.locale (pdf/mod.rs:168->page_dims us=Letter). Guaranteed us mismatch vs EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:120 'always agree'. But wrong page-size is formatting, no data-loss/crash: medium.

##### `export-b-002` · export-b · _orphans_

**Proprietary Microsoft Calibri font files (calibri.ttf + calibrib.ttf, ~3.2 MB) remain committed as printpdf-era orphans that the Typst migration's 'delete the entire printpdf stack' promise should have removed; nothing in the current code loads them.**

REQ-01028 (PR #82) originally 'bundled Calibri fonts' for the printpdf renderer. REQ-04031 (PR #228) then migrated all PDF export to Typst, vendored Carlito precisely as a 'Calibri-metric-compatible' OFL substitute, and promised to 'delete the entire printpdf stack and its dependencies'. That deletion was incomplete for the font assets: apps/desktop/src-tauri/fonts/ still ships calibri.ttf (1.6 MB) and calibrib.ttf (1.5 MB). Verification shows they are fully dead: no include_bytes! references them (world.rs bundles only Carlito/Inter/Source Serif 4/Manrope), the Tauri bundle resources array is empty so they are not shipped to users, build.rs has no font logic, and the only 'Calibri' references in code are system-font NAME strings for DOCX fallback (docx_renderer.rs:39-41 map FontFamily -> "Calibri" for Word to substitute — they never read the .ttf). download-fonts.ps1 does not even fetch Calibri. fonts/README.md (line 3) still documents these files as the PDF-export Calibri source, which is stale — PDF export no longer uses Calibri at all. Committing proprietary Microsoft fonts to the repo is a licensing concern and 3.2 MB of dead weight; the whole point of vendoring Carlito was to avoid shipping Calibri.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/fonts/README.md:3` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/docx_renderer.rs:39` @ `6b224c9c4b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04031, REQ-01028
- **Fix:** Delete apps/desktop/src-tauri/fonts/calibri.ttf and calibrib.ttf (proprietary, unused) and update fonts/README.md to drop the 'may optionally contain the Calibri font files' section, since PDF export now uses Carlito via Typst and DOCX only names 'Calibri' as a system-font fallback string.

##### `export-b-004` · export-b · _consistency_

**The normative EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md Fonts section still describes the bundle as 'exactly two font families (Carlito + Noto Sans)' and credits Cyrillic coverage to Noto Sans, but the code bundles four families (Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope) and NO Noto Sans — Cyrillic actually comes from Inter.**

REQ-04031 originally vendored 'Carlito/Noto fonts', and EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md — declared the normative export contract 'locked by tests' (REQ-07023) — documents this at multiple points: line 288 'Two font families are vendored', line 294 lists 'Noto Sans (Latin + Cyrillic subsets) | all templates (body fallback)', line 297 'Noto Sans covers Latin/Cyrillic scripts', and line 266 'fonts (Carlito + Noto Sans) support Latin + Cyrillic only'. As premium templates were added, the bundle diverged: world.rs (lines 65-81) now include_bytes! Carlito (4 faces) + Inter (2) + Source Serif 4 (3) + Manrope (2) = FOUR families, and Noto Sans is not bundled at all. The .typ templates still list 'Noto Sans' but only as the final entry of fallback chains always preceded by bundled Carlito/Inter, so it is a dead fallback (no tofu, no runtime break). The doc is nonetheless materially wrong on the normative contract: it omits Inter/Source Serif 4/Manrope (which are PRIMARY fonts for Atelier/Meridian/Portrait/Throughline), claims a non-bundled font (Noto Sans), and misattributes Cyrillic coverage — Cyrillic (the supported 'ru' generation language, REQ-16470) is actually served by Inter (world.rs:71 'Inter — good Unicode / Cyrillic coverage'), not Noto Sans. Latent risk: the doc's own CJK plan says 'bundle Noto Sans CJK' to extend a Noto base that does not exist, and anyone trusting the doc could drop Inter believing Carlito+Noto cover Cyrillic, regressing Russian output to tofu.

- **Evidence:** `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:288` @ `60c00dea1b` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:297` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/world.rs:65` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16700, REQ-04031, REQ-07023
- **Fix:** Update the EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md Fonts section to list the actual bundled set (Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope with their template usages), remove Noto Sans, and correct the Cyrillic-coverage sentence to credit Inter. Reconcile the CJK note (lines 266-269) accordingly. Optionally drop the dead 'Noto Sans' fallback tokens from the .typ templates.

##### `i18n-002` · i18n · _orphans_

**The persistent i18n:extract drift is dominated by ~45 support.diagnostics._/support.recovery._ keys referenced only by nine dead diagnostic components that nothing renders (the live SupportPage is an FAQ accordion).**

The 503KB i18n:extract drift, once bucketed, is: 3 unresolvable dynamic placeholders (${base}.details, jobs.boards.<id>, jobs.trust.flags.${flag} — expected/advisory), 17 plural _one/*other suffixes that are benign (the app uses manual pluralization via base keys + {{count}}; i18next 26.3.4 falls back to the base key, verified: t('count',{count:2}) => '2 selected'), 5 genuinely-missing live keys (finding i18n-001), and ~45 support.diagnostics.*/support.recovery._ keys. Those 45 come entirely from nine components under features/support/components/ (ScrapingDiagnostics, RecoveryTools, SystemHealthDashboard, AIRuntimeDiagnostics, PerformanceMemory, LogsDiagnostics, DocumentTroubleshooting, KnowledgeBase, ContactFeedback). They have existed since the initial release (e2d44384) but are imported and rendered by NOTHING — the live SupportPage (rendered) builds only an FAQ accordion from support-data.ts (SupportPage line 22). They are orphans of a superseded tabbed-support design (the vestigial support.tabs keys corroborate it) and carry fabricated data (hardcoded '98%' success rates; ScrapingDiagnostics line 37 still references the retired Workday board). Because they never render, users never see the raw keys — but they are why the advisory i18n key-check perpetually drifts, which masks real missing-key regressions like i18n-001. REQ-09020 explicitly requires removing dead keys and validating keys by grepping their t() call sites at review time.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/ScrapingDiagnostics/index.tsx:89` @ `e2d4438489` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/RecoveryTools/index.tsx:49` @ `e2d4438489` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/SupportPage/index.tsx:22` @ `e2d4438489`
- **Requirements:** REQ-09020, REQ-06050, REQ-06013
- **Fix:** Delete the nine unused features/support/components/* diagnostic components and their vestigial support.tabs / support.diagnostics.* / support.recovery.* keys — or, if the tabbed diagnostics are still intended, wire them into SupportPage and add their ~45 keys to en+de (and drop the retired-Workday/fabricated data). Removing the dead code clears the bulk of the i18n:extract drift so the advisory check regains signal for real gaps.

##### `ipc-b-002` · ipc-b · _orphans_

**Two Electron-era file-import Tauri commands are registered but have no renderer caller — `dialog.openFiles` (native picker -> paths) and `resume::extract_resume(path)` — orphaned by the transport-neutral bytes model, and extract_resume exposes an unvalidated arbitrary-path file read.**

REQ-00031 (commit bd7f1c4) established the transport-neutral name+bytes import model (the sandboxed renderer sends file bytes, not server-side paths), and REQ-00039 (commit 97cbad8) made `resume_extract_text` the bytes-based extraction command. The live path today is `resume.extractText` -> `resume_extract_text` with file selection via HTML `<input type=file>` (used by AnalyzePage/useFileUpload/AIGeneratePage). The older path-based pair survived as dead IPC surface: (1) `commands::resume::extract_resume(path: String)` is registered in the invoke_handler (lib.rs:800) but has NO tauri-client binding and NO renderer/raw-invoke caller (`git grep extract_resume` in renderer+client is empty; the `resume` contract only exposes `extractText`); (2) `commands::dialog::dialog_open_files` is registered (lib.rs:848) and returns `string[]` paths, is bound in the tauri-client (dialog/dialog.ts:4), but its only renderer reference is the mock-client stub (mock-client.ts:311) — no production code calls `api.dialog.openFiles`. Both are the Electron-era 'native open dialog -> path -> extract(path)' flow, unreachable under the bytes model. extract_resume additionally accepts a raw filesystem path with no traversal scope-check (contra the REQ-16039 rule that every #[tauri::command] validate input and scope-check fs/path access) and performs file I/O, making it latent arbitrary-file-read surface reachable via a raw `invoke('extract_resume', { path })`.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/resume.rs:13` @ `bd7f1c461f` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:848` @ `bd7f1c461f` · `apps/desktop/src/tauri-client/namespaces/dialog/dialog.ts:4` @ `97cbad8d34`
- **Requirements:** REQ-00031, REQ-00039, REQ-16039
- **Fix:** Delete both orphaned commands and their bindings: drop the `commands::resume::extract_resume` #[tauri::command] (keep the internal `extraction::extract_resume` fn if it is still called in-process) and the entire `dialog` namespace (command registration, contract, tauri-client namespace, mock stub). If a native file-picker/path flow is genuinely planned, wire a real consumer and add path traversal/scope validation to extract_resume per REQ-16039.

##### `p2-b2-shared-schemas-001` · p2-b2-shared-schemas · _boundary_

**The shared `AppMetrics`/`ProcessMetric` type is a stale Electron-era shape that the Tauri Rust `system_get_metrics` producer never returns, so the dashboard's renderer-memory readout silently never renders.**

REQ-00024 / PR #30 (b6184d0a, pre-Tauri) introduced `AppMetrics`+`ProcessMetric` in packages/shared/src/types/index.ts modelled directly on Electron's `app.getAppMetrics()` (fields `processes[].cpuUsage.percentCPUUsage`, `memory.workingSetSize`, plus `boot`/`startupMs`/`jobQueue`/`snapshotAt`) and the `getMetrics(): Promise<AppMetrics>` contract. REQ-00057 / PR #61 (d5da71f2) reimplemented `system_get_metrics` in Rust via sysinfo, returning a completely different Tauri-native object `{shell, uptime, memoryMb, totalMemoryMb, cpuPercent, cpuCount, cpuBrand, cpuName, gpus}` with NONE of the AppMetrics fields; Electron was then deleted (53a331170) but the shared type was never trimmed. `use-system-resources.ts` already reads the REAL fields (`memoryMb`, `gpus`) via its own local inline type, proving the `AppMetrics` contract is fiction. But `AISystemStatus/index.tsx` still trusts the stale contract and reads `metrics?.processes?.find(p=>p.type==='renderer')?.memory?.workingSetSize`; since Rust returns no `processes`, `rendererMem` is always undefined and the `{rendererMem != null && ... MB}` block (lines 177-181) never renders. Any future consumer reading `boot`/`jobQueue`/`snapshotAt`/`startupMs` would likewise silently get undefined.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/system/mod.rs:482` @ `d5da71f2f9` · `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts:166` @ `b6184d0ab9` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/dashboard/components/AISystemStatus/index.tsx:31` @ `b6184d0ab9`
- **Requirements:** REQ-00024, REQ-00057
- **Fix:** Retire the Electron-era `AppMetrics`/`BootMetrics`/`ProcessMetric` types (and the `getMetrics(): Promise<AppMetrics>` contract) and replace them with the actual Tauri `system_get_metrics` shape that `use-system-resources.ts` already reads (shell/uptime/memoryMb/totalMemoryMb/cpuPercent/cpuCount/cpuBrand/cpuName/gpus). Update `AISystemStatus/index.tsx` to stop reading the non-existent `processes` field — either drop the dead renderer-memory line or source it from a real field the Rust producer emits.

##### `p2-b5-translations-seam-002` · p2-b5-translations-seam · _boundary_

**Two dead support components (ScrapingDiagnostics, RecoveryTools) reference ~45 t() keys absent from the locales; the components are exported but never mounted anywhere.**

ScrapingDiagnostics (25 missing keys under support.diagnostics._: scrapingSessionStatus, sessionTokens(Desc), cookieStorage(Desc), rateLimitTracker(Desc), proxyConfiguration(Desc), commonScrapingIssues, rateLimitingIssue, rateLimitingSolution1-4, captchaIssue, captchaSolution1-4, sessionExpiredIssue, sessionExpiredSolution1-4) and RecoveryTools (20 missing keys under support.recovery._: rebuildIndexes/clearCache/resetDatabase/reloadRuntime/unloadModels/resetConfig/clearOcrCache/reindexDocs/resetSessions/clearQueue Title+Desc) each mix a few keys that DO exist in en (e.g. support.recovery.vectorDatabaseRecovery, support.diagnostics.providerHealth) with dozens that do not. An unfiltered grep across apps/ and packages/ finds no importer for either component — the only reference to each is its own `export function` line — so they are dead code and the missing keys cannot break users today. They align with REQ-08041 ('build a support/diagnostics dashboard'), which was left in 'documented' status with no PR: the UI was partially built and its locale keys never completed. Impact: real used->defined seam gaps that would render raw keys if the dashboard is ever wired up, and they permanently inflate the advisory extract diff (finding 003).

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/RecoveryTools/index.tsx:18` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/RecoveryTools/index.tsx:57` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/ScrapingDiagnostics/index.tsx:48` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:2234` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-08041, REQ-09020
- **Fix:** Either delete the two dead components (they are unmounted; REQ-09020 says remove dead UI rather than translate it) or finish REQ-08041: wire them into SupportPage and add all 45 keys to en/de. Do not leave them half-built — they are the bulk of the advisory-check noise.

##### `p2-b5-translations-seam-003` · p2-b5-translations-seam · _boundary_

**The extract config's `sort: true` contradicts the manually-grouped committed locale files, so the advisory CI guard can never be the 'clean no-op' its own comment promises and its missing-key warning is permanently red (hence ignored).**

i18next.config.ts:24 sets `sort: true`, but the committed en/de files are hand-ordered by feature (common, app, nav, build, models, shortcuts, aiSetup, ...) not alphabetically. Running the extractor re-sorts the whole file alphabetically (${base}, ai, aiGenerate, app, applications, autopilot, ...), which is why the recorded advisory diff is a full-file rewrite (~5.4k added / ~5.2k removed lines for a 2.9k-line file) rather than the handful of added lines the real 50 missing keys represent. The config comment at line 12 claims the check 'stays a clean no-op when the locales are in sync', and quality.yml's i18n job (`continue-on-error: true`, decides via `git diff --quiet` after extract) even labels its warning '(missing keys / sort drift)'. Because sort drift keeps the diff permanently non-empty AND the job never fails the build, the missing-key signal is indistinguishable from noise and gets ignored — the mechanism by which finding 001's live user-facing gaps accumulated undetected.

- **Evidence:** `packages/translations/i18next.config.ts:24` @ `977cea621f` · `packages/translations/i18next.config.ts:12` @ `977cea621f` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:1` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `.github/workflows/quality.yml:190` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-06050, REQ-09020
- **Fix:** Make the guard's diff meaningful: either set `sort: false` and stop reformatting, or commit the files in the extractor's sorted order once and keep them sorted so a real missing key produces a small, reviewable diff. Only then does the advisory warning become actionable.

##### `p2-b7-events-deeplink-002` · p2-b7-events-deeplink · _boundary_

**Rust emits `scrape:progress` and `scrape:item`, but no renderer listener subscribes to either channel — and for the autopilot scrape path these are the ONLY delivery mechanism, so its live progress/items are dropped.**

The SCRAPE_EVENTS namespace is a registered, typed channel (packages/shared/src/events/scrape.ts defines SCRAPE_EVENTS + ScrapeProgressEvent {jobId,progress} + ScrapeItemEvent {jobId,item}; events.rs mirrors the consts; index.ts:70-71 + the lock test enforce them), yet an exhaustive sweep of every renderer `listen()` call finds NO subscriber for `scrape:progress` or `scrape:item` — the only scrape-side namespace (tauri-client/namespaces/scrape) exposes command methods only, no event listeners. autopilot_helpers/mod.rs emits BOTH channels as the sole callback path (on_progress -> SCRAPE_PROGRESS at line 58; on_item -> SCRAPE_ITEM at line 69) with no jobs:event fallback, so an autopilot run's per-item stream and progress are serialized and emitted into the void. In the manual scrape command (commands/scrape.rs) the same on_progress ALSO calls job_progress (jobs:event) and on_item emits jobs:event `job.stream` — the channel the renderer actually consumes (jobs.onEvent) — so there `scrape:progress` is a redundant duplicate emit and `scrape:item` is never emitted at all. REQ-03084 documented the intent to 'stream results to the renderer via app.emit()'; that was implemented on jobs:event, leaving the scrape:* channel orphaned. No user-facing breakage (autopilot surfaces progress via autopilot:step and is notify-only), but it is a dead registered channel plus wasted per-item serialization each autopilot scrape.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/autopilot_helpers/mod.rs:69` @ `7d2d759968` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/autopilot_helpers/mod.rs:58` @ `7d2d759968` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/events.rs:18` @ `47111d9a08`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03084, REQ-12006
- **Fix:** Pick one side. If a live scrape/autopilot progress UI is wanted, add a renderer listener for scrape:progress/scrape:item (a scrape namespace onProgress/onItem). Otherwise delete the orphaned SCRAPE_EVENTS channel: remove ScrapeProgressEvent/ScrapeItemEvent + the registry/AppEvents/lock-test entries, the SCRAPE_PROGRESS/SCRAPE_ITEM consts, the redundant scrape:progress emit in commands/scrape.rs, and the two autopilot_helpers emits — consolidating on jobs:event, which the renderer already consumes.

##### `p2-b7-events-deeplink-003` · p2-b7-events-deeplink · _boundary_

**Rust emits `boards:login-status` (per-step login progress) during in-app board browser login, but no renderer listener subscribes — the connect flow only awaits the final command result, so all intermediate login status is dropped.**

boards_login_with_browser installs an on_status callback that emits `boards:login-status` with `{boardId, status}` for each login step (commands/boards.rs:20), and the channel is a fully registered typed seam (packages/shared/src/events/boards.ts BOARDS_EVENTS.loginStatus + BoardsLoginStatusEvent; events.rs:19; index.ts:72 + lock test). But no renderer `listen()` call subscribes to it anywhere — the board-connect service hooks (use-boards.ts useBoardConnect / useLinkedInConnect) just `await api.boards.connect(...)` / `api.linkedin.connect()` and read the single terminal `{connected}` result, then refetch status. The progress strings the Rust side goes out of its way to emit are never surfaced (the user sees only a pending spinner with no step detail). Classic one-sided seam: the emitter was wired for a progress UI that the renderer never implemented (or that was dropped in a connect-flow refactor). Login still completes, so no hard breakage — a silent partial: an intended progress channel with zero consumers.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/boards.rs:20` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/events.rs:19` @ `47111d9a08`
- **Requirements:** REQ-11040, REQ-11031
- **Fix:** Either subscribe to boards:login-status in the board-connect flow to surface per-step login progress (a boards namespace onLoginStatus listener feeding the connect UI), or, if the terminal result is sufficient, drop the on_status emit, BOARDS_EVENTS.loginStatus, the BoardsLoginStatusEvent type, the BOARDS_LOGIN_STATUS const, and their AppEvents/lock-test entries.

##### `p2-b8-export-contract-001` · p2-b8-export-contract · _boundary_

**The EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md Fonts contract still says only two families (Carlito + Noto Sans) are vendored, but the Typst world bundles four (Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope) and never includes Noto Sans.**

docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md (the self-declared 'normative reference / contract', last-updated 2026-06-11) states 'Two font families are vendored and embedded via include_bytes!' and lists Carlito + 'Noto Sans (Latin + Cyrillic subsets) | all templates (body fallback)', and bases its Cyrillic/CJK rationale on Noto Sans. The actual Typst world (world.rs) embeds 11 faces across FOUR families - Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope - and no Noto Sans at all. This has been true since the Typst migration (#228, 60c00dea): the same commit's world.rs already bundled Inter/Source-Serif-4/Manrope while the doc already claimed Carlito+Noto. Cyrillic coverage actually comes from Inter (world.rs comment: 'Inter - good Unicode / Cyrillic coverage'), not the doc's claimed Noto Sans. The templates still carry a ghost 'Noto Sans' entry at the tail of their font stacks (e.g. classic.typ) that resolves to nothing because Noto Sans is not registered in the FontBook. Ledger REQ-16700 ('Vendor and embed EXACTLY TWO font families ... Carlito ... Noto Sans for Latin/Cyrillic') is status 'partial' - confirming the implementation diverged from the documented two-family/Noto intent. Risk: a maintainer trusting the contract could prune Inter believing 'Noto Sans covers Cyrillic', silently breaking ru rendering; or duplicate-bundle a font already present; and the doc omits three fonts (Inter/Source Serif 4/Manrope) that are actually shipped and used as PRIMARY fonts (SwissMinimal=Manrope, Academic=Source Serif 4, Meridian/Portrait=Inter).

- **Evidence:** `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:294` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/world.rs:72` @ `04e6e78aed` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:288` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/templates/classic.typ:43` @ `04e6e78aed`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16700, REQ-16225
- **Fix:** Update the EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md Fonts table to list the four vendored families actually embedded in world.rs (Carlito, Inter, Source Serif 4, Manrope), attribute Latin/Cyrillic coverage to Inter/Carlito (not the never-bundled Noto Sans), and correct the CJK-limitation premise to name the real bundled fonts. Separately, drop the dead 'Noto Sans' tail from the template font stacks (classic/atelier/lebenslauf/letter) or actually bundle it - pick one so doc, code, and template stacks agree.

##### `p2-b8-export-contract-002` · p2-b8-export-contract · _boundary_

**EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md claims 'typst-svg is a dev-dependency, never shipped' and 'confined to the test function', but typst-svg is a [dependencies] runtime crate used in production by the live-preview render path.**

The 'Cover-letter template previews' section of docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md asserts the SVG path is a hard-wall test isolation - 'typst and typst_svg crates stay confined to the test function; no typst types appear in production code paths. typst-svg is a dev-dependency, never shipped.' That was true when only the ignored preview-generation test used SVG, but the later live-preview feature (ledger REQ-12029/REQ-06044) moved SVG into production: engine.rs::compile_and_svg (NOT #[cfg(test)]) calls typst_svg::svg and is invoked by the production render_resume_svg_pages / render_letter_svg_pages, wired into documents_render_preview_images. Cargo.toml now declares typst-svg under [dependencies] (its own inline comment: 'typst-svg moved to [dependencies] - it now backs the live-preview render path'), and ledger REQ-14044 pins typst-svg as part of the runtime typst crate family. So the doc's dev-dependency/never-shipped/test-confined claim is a stale carry-over that directly contradicts both the shipped Cargo manifest and the production engine code. Blast radius is bounded (the load-bearing invariant - no typst types in pub signatures - still holds, since compile_and_svg returns Vec<String>), but a security/dependency reviewer trusting 'never shipped' would mis-scope the shipped binary's crate/attack surface.

- **Evidence:** `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:202` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml:153` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/engine.rs:210` @ `04e6e78aed`
- **Requirements:** REQ-12029, REQ-14044, REQ-06044
- **Fix:** Amend the 'Cover-letter template previews' section: keep the test-preview description but delete/replace the 'typst-svg is a dev-dependency, never shipped / confined to the test function' claim with the fact that typst-svg is a shipped [dependencies] runtime crate backing the live-preview path (render_resume_svg_pages / render_letter_svg_pages via compile_and_svg), cross-referencing the 'Live preview' section and Cargo.toml.

##### `p2-b9-001` · p2-b9-declared-boundaries · _boundary_

**docs/architecture-rules.md's L0-L3 layer model still lists two phantom modules (browser in L0, conversations in L1) that no longer exist and were dropped from the enforcing test's LAYER table, breaking the doc's own self-declared 'duplicated verbatim' invariant.**

The doc asserts (lines 31-34) that its L0-L3 list is 'the single source of truth and is duplicated verbatim as the LAYER table at the top of tests/architecture.rs.' It is not verbatim: docs L0 (line 28, header line 39) still lists `browser` and docs L1 (line 24) still lists `conversations`, but tests/architecture.rs L0 (lines 24-34) and L1 (lines 35-55) contain neither, and no `browser`/`conversations` module exists under src/. `conversations` was deleted in PR #328 (cc14afdb) whose own commit body states it dropped the L1/R2/R3 conversations allowlist entries -- yet the diff touched apps/tauri/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs (-3) and did NOT touch docs/architecture-rules.md, leaving the doc stale. `browser` was a genuine L0 module in both doc and test at arch creation (#195, verified: test L0 held "browser") and has since been removed from code+test but retained in the doc. A maintainer trusting the doc believes browser/conversations are live layered modules; the doc's headline parity guarantee is false.

- **Evidence:** `docs/architecture-rules.md:24` @ `cc14afdb39` · `docs/architecture-rules.md:28` @ `2ed31d2838` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs:35` @ `cc14afdb39`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03033, REQ-06048, REQ-16044, REQ-16069
- **Fix:** Sync docs/architecture-rules.md to tests/architecture.rs: remove `browser` from the L0 list (line 28) and header (line 39), and remove `conversations` from the L1 list (line 24). Better, add a CI check that diffs the doc's fenced layer list against the L0-L3 const arrays so removals can't silently desync (the doc already promises they are identical).

##### `p2-b9-002` · p2-b9-declared-boundaries · _contradiction_

**The R2/R3/R4 exception tables in docs/architecture-rules.md contradict the enforced allowlists in tests/architecture.rs -- most sharply R4, where the doc documents an ai_provider env-read exception that the test has removed (R4_ALLOW is now empty).**

R4 is a direct contradiction: docs (line 132) claim `commands/ai_provider/{cli_agent,ollama}` are allowlisted to read env (OLLAMA_HOST/<AGENT>_BIN), but tests/architecture.rs declares `const R4_ALLOW: &[&str] = &[]` (line 352) with a comment that env is now fully centralized in platform::config -- and grep confirms commands/ai_provider/ contains no std::env::var/AJH_DATA_DIR today. At doc creation (#195, 2ed31d2) that same R4_ALLOW did list "commands/ai_provider/cli_agent/mod.rs" and "commands/ai_provider/ollama.rs", so the exception was real and was later refactored away in the test only. A dev trusting the doc who adds an env read to ai_provider would hit a surprising arch-test failure. R3: docs (line 131) claim '9 genuine owners' including removed `conversations` while omitting the real test owners applications/mod.rs, contact_profile/mod.rs, and scraping/board_login/import.rs (test R3_ALLOW, lines 317-333, has 11 entries). R2: docs (line 130) list removed `conversations` and over-claim `cover_letter/{mod,leakage,research}` when only cover_letter/research/mod.rs is in the test R2_ALLOW (mod.rs and leakage.rs carry no Tauri markers today). The test -- the actual gate -- is correct; the doc, which bills itself as 'the formal, testable contract', is stale in three of its rule rows.

- **Evidence:** `docs/architecture-rules.md:132` @ `2ed31d2838` · `docs/architecture-rules.md:131` @ `cc14afdb39` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs:352` @ `2ed31d2838`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03048, REQ-06048, REQ-12053, REQ-16067
- **Fix:** Sync docs/architecture-rules.md rows R2/R3/R4 to tests/architecture.rs: drop the R4 ai_provider env exception (now clean/empty), remove conversations from R2 and R3 and cover_letter/{mod,leakage} from R2, and add applications/contact_profile/scraping-board_login-import to R3 with a corrected count. Ideally render the exception tables from the test's *_ALLOW consts so the prose can never diverge from enforcement.

The documented capability contradicts the wired reality; validators remain the only enforcement for every provider.

##### `p2-contra-ci-release-001` · p2-contra-ci-release · _contradiction_

**The deployment-rules skill still states releases are 'Automated via semantic-release on push to main', contradicting the implemented manual-dispatch-only release gate (REQ-10015).**

The drift was even recorded as REQ-16404 and marked superseded_by REQ-10015, yet .claude/skills/deployment-rules/SKILL.md line 8 — the exact doc project-steward loads for /prepare-release — was never corrected and still instructs the pre-#418 on-push automation model.

##### `p2-contra-export-resume-001` · p2-contra-export-resume · _contradiction_

The resume-export-standards skill (baked 2026-06-19, PR #433) states exported documents must have tagged structure, logical reading order, and language/Title metadata, citing the EU European Accessibility Act as a binding driver. The Typst migration (PR #228, REQ-04031) never enabled any PDF standard: compile_and_export uses PdfOptions::default(), which emits untagged, non-PDF/UA output, and no subsequent requirement in the 111-entry topic timeline implements or explicitly defers accessibility (unlike CJK, which got an explicit deferral entry). Resolution is unresolved: the standard is documented as current review doctrine while the code has never followed it.

- **Fix:** Either enable Typst's tagged/PDF-UA output (typst-pdf PdfOptions standards field, available in the pinned 0.14 family) or add an explicit documented deferral entry (like REQ-16225 for CJK) so the standard stops reading as an unmet mandate.

Current code: BOARD_IDS and SCRAPERS no longer contain any of the five (ScrapeBoardsRequestSchema rejects 'indeed'), yet packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:1745-1747 (and the de mirror) still ship jobs.boards.indeed/xing/glassdoor while jobs.boards.stepstone/workday are gone — the only dynamic consumers are t(`jobs.boards.${id}`) sites fed from BOARD_IDS or scrape results, which can never emit retired ids. Additionally apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/lib/search-index.ts:273-276 still lists 'indeed','glassdoor','xing','stepstone' as search keywords for the boards-login section although only linkedin remains an active auth-capable board and stepstone was never in AUTH_CAPABLE_BOARDS. This is a silent partial of an implemented-status requirement and also violates the documented dead-key rule REQ-09020; either keep all five labels defensively for legacy data or strip all five plus the ghost search keywords.

- **Evidence:** `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:1747` @ `bd3c34424d` · `packages/translations/src/locales/de/translation.json:1761` @ `11ab8c0a38` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/lib/search-index.ts:276` @ `bd3c34424d`
- **Requirements:** REQ-11042, REQ-11050, REQ-16631, REQ-09020
- **Fix:** Pick one policy: (a) delete jobs.boards.indeed/xing/glassdoor from en+de and the retired-board keywords ('indeed','glassdoor','xing','stepstone') from settings search-index.ts, or (b) if labels are kept defensively for legacy stored jobs/autopilots, restore stepstone/workday keys too and document the exception against REQ-09020/REQ-16631.

##### `p2-contra-prompts-001` · p2-contra-prompts · _contradiction_

**REQ-15039 requires ALL AI-generated application text to pass as authentic human writing, but the Rust agent tool prompts got only a hand-inlined subset of the humanize rules, so agent-drafted cover letters/resumes still emit the top detector tells.**

PR #563 (169f7ae8) composed the full ANTI_AI_TELL_LEXICAL/PROSE + HUMANIZE tiers into every TS builder (cover letter, resume, answers, referral, email, rewrite) and touched agent/tools.rs, but the compact Rust prompts (COVER_LETTER_SYSTEM, RESUME_SYSTEM, INTERVIEW_QUESTIONS_SYSTEM) only received cadence-variety and cliche-cut lines. The AI-vocabulary ban list (delve/leverage/robust/...), promotional-adjective ban (passionate/results-driven/...), and the em-dash hard ban — which natural-voice.ts itself calls the biggest AI tells — are absent from the agent path. REQ-16238/REQ-16664 acknowledge the compact-Rust-prompt drift risk, but 'ALL generated text' (REQ-15039) and 'compact subset' (REQ-16664) resolve as mixed: the same feature (draft a cover letter) is humanized on the TS surface and only partially on the agent surface.

REQ-15040 asked to wire the previously dead Output Tone setting 'into the generators' via toneDirective. In the shipped code (PR #563) toneDirective is called by buildResumeSystemPrompt, buildCoverLetterSystemPrompt, and buildApplicationAnswerSystemPrompt only (generation.ts reads outputTone at exactly those three call sites). buildReferralPrompt/buildReferralImprovePrompt (REQ-12062) and buildApplicationEmailPrompt (REQ-14008) take no tone parameter, yet the toneDirective doc comment states 'prose surfaces (cover letter, application answers, referral, email) omit it and get the full directive'. ##### `p2-contra-renderer-features-001` · p2-contra-renderer-features · _contradiction_

**REQ-16239 ('hybrid semantic search reachable only via Cmd/Ctrl+K') is marked implemented, but the current code has neither a Cmd+K accelerator, nor an emitter, nor any search UI - only a dead cross-layer plumbing chain and a stale code comment advertising the shortcut.**

REQ-02038 built a hybrid semantic SearchPage; REQ-16239 later moved that search off the sidebar onto a Cmd/Ctrl+K shortcut (shipped in #252 'move search to a shortcut'). The search surface was subsequently removed, leaving orphaned plumbing: `SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE` is declared in events.rs but NEVER emitted (lib.rs registers CmdOrCtrl+1..7 nav accelerators, no +K), the tauri-client `shortcuts.onCommandPalette` listener is wired, and `useCommandPaletteShortcut` exists as a renderer hook with ZERO consumers. No search route or search component exists. The SpotlightTour still carries a comment telling users search 'is a Cmd/Ctrl+K shortcut, surfaced in the keyboard-shortcuts cheat sheet'. Net: a capability the ledger records as implemented is unreachable, and dead command-palette-named plumbing survives across events.rs -> shortcuts.ts -> use-support.ts -> mock-client.ts.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/services/use-support/use-support.ts:76` @ `a7e6cb5c5d` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/events.rs:20` @ `2d953096da` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/onboarding/SpotlightTour/index.tsx:26` @ `2d953096da`
