- **Fix:** Either restore a Cmd/Ctrl+K search surface (registering the accelerator to emit SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE and a consumer that opens a search modal) or delete the whole dead chain (events.rs const, shortcuts.ts listener, useCommandPaletteShortcut hook, mock) and the stale SpotlightTour comment; mark REQ-16239 as not-implemented / superseded accordingly.

##### `p2-contra-renderer-features-002` · p2-contra-renderer-features · _contradiction_

**REQ-16011 makes keyword-only (semanticScoring=false) THE DEFAULT scoring path, but the Rust scoring default is the opposite - semantic_enabled_bit(None) returns 1 (semantic ON), enshrined by a passing test, and the agent match_resume tool realizes it by deserializing LLM args that routinely omit the flag.**

The renderer (preferences default false, always sends semanticScoringEnabled) and autopilot (build_found_job is explicitly embedding-free, keyword coverage only) both correctly default to keyword-only per REQ-16011 and the opt-in-embeddings rule REQ-06056. But the backend fallback contradicts them: `semantic_enabled_bit` returns 1 for None, and a unit test cements 'default (unset) -> enabled'. The agent match_resume_handler does `serde_json::from_value(args)?` directly on model-supplied args (tool schema marks semanticScoringEnabled optional); when the LLM omits it, the agent silently runs embedding-based scoring against the user's opt-in preference, incurring the token cost REQ-06056 warns about. This is code following the OLDER ask (REQ-02038 semantic 0.6/0.4 default) at the default layer while the user-facing paths follow the newer REQ-16011 - a mixed, incoherent resolution.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/match_resume.rs:44` @ `74199796c5` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/match_resume.rs:572` @ `74199796c5` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/agent/tools.rs:219` @ `a6face0339`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16011, REQ-06056, REQ-02038
- **Fix:** Flip semantic_enabled_bit so None (unset) defaults to keyword-only (0), matching REQ-16011/REQ-06056; update the test to assert 'default -> keyword-only'. The agent tool then inherits the correct opt-in default instead of silently embedding.

##### `p2-contra-scraping-001` · p2-contra-scraping · _contradiction_

**Board-count docs are stale and mutually inconsistent: the SCRAPERS/BOARD_IDS registry has 23 boards but README says 16, API.md and ARCHITECTURE_STATUS say 21, and API.md's 'canonical BOARD_IDS' list omits workable and comeet.**

PR #535 (commit 47111d9a) added the workable and comeet boards, growing the registry 21->23 (SCRAPERS in boards/mod.rs and BOARD_IDS in packages/shared both list 23, including workable+comeet). That commit updated only docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md (now correctly '23 boards'); README.md:91 still says 'scrapes 16 job boards', README.md:513 and docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70 say 21, and docs/API.md:406 claims to mirror the canonical BOARD_IDS list yet enumerates only 21 ids, silently omitting workable and comeet. This directly violates the standing rule REQ-16059 ('board counts must always come from the SCRAPERS registry, never a copied number in docs') and the REQ-14033 checklist (API.md/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS board-count sync left to the doc-sync pass, which did not run for #535). The '16' in README is doubly stale — it was the momentary post-ADR-026 retirement count before boards were re-added (16->20->21->23).

- **Evidence:** `docs/API.md:406` @ `47111d9a08` · `README.md:91` @ `47111d9a08` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70` @ `47111d9a08` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/mod.rs:57` @ `47111d9a08`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16456, REQ-16059, REQ-15001, REQ-14033, REQ-16631
- **Fix:** Sync the copied counts to the live registry (23): README.md:91 (16->23) and :513, docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70 (21->23), and docs/API.md:406 (add 'workable' and 'comeet', 21->23). Better: derive the API.md board-id list and counts programmatically from BOARD_IDS/SCRAPERS so they cannot drift again.

##### `p2-contra-security-001` · p2-contra-security · _contradiction_

**The formal 'the only outbound calls are to the AI provider + an opt-in web search' privacy guarantee in SECURITY.md and README is contradicted by a shipped opt-in Clearbit third-party egress.**

PR #499 (be378fb5, 2026-06-25) added company-logo enrichment: when the fetchCompanyLogos preference is on, CompanyAvatar sends each viewed posting's company name to the Clearbit data broker (autocomplete.clearbit.com for the query, logo.clearbit.com for the image), and the Tauri CSP was widened to grant both hosts. The feature is opt-in / default-off and disclosed at its settings toggle, but the top-level privacy guarantees in README.md:93 and SECURITY.md:50 make an EXHAUSTIVE claim that the only outbound calls are to the user-configured AI provider plus an explicitly-enabled web search. Company-logo enrichment is a third egress to a third party not covered by that enumeration, so a privacy-conscious user (the stated target audience) auditing SECURITY.md is not told about the Clearbit data-broker egress. The documented rule (REQ-16455/REQ-16108) and the shipped feature coexist unresolved: code follows the newer feature while the older privacy guarantee was never updated to disclose it.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:30` @ `be378fb563` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/services/use-company-logo/use-company-logo.ts:31` @ `be378fb563` · `SECURITY.md:50` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `README.md:93` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16455, REQ-16108
- **Fix:** Update SECURITY.md:50 and README.md:93 to disclose the opt-in Clearbit company-logo enrichment as an additional third-party egress alongside web search (or scope the 'only outbound calls' claim to the default configuration and name the logo lookup explicitly), so the exhaustive privacy guarantee matches shipped behavior.

- **Evidence:** `eslint.config.mjs:288` @ `d134ee9eca` · `CLAUDE.md:52` @ `d134ee9eca` · `packages/ui/src/index.ts:31` @ `d134ee9eca`
- **Requirements:** REQ-01017, REQ-01022, REQ-08010, REQ-16439
- **Fix:** Rename SelectDropdown to Dropdown across all rule/instruction surfaces (CLAUDE.md, eslint.config.mjs messages, agent/editor rule files, Form.tsx comment), drop the dead '@/components/ui/SelectDropdown' deep-import ban entry, and mark REQ-01017/REQ-01022 superseded by REQ-08010.

##### `p2-contra-ux-002` · p2-contra-ux · _contradiction_

**REQ-16221 (status 'partial') asserts 'the macOS equivalent is deferred' for live OS-accent updates, but the macOS accent watcher was fully implemented and is present in current code.**

REQ-16221 (null-date, status 'partial') documents live OS-accent theming for Windows (WinRT UISettings::ColorValuesChanged) and states 'the macOS equivalent is deferred'. REQ-08060 (2026-06-14, 'implemented') explicitly implemented the macOS equivalent 'in the current PR' (#381). Commit 74199796 created platform/accent_watcher.rs (238 lines) including the macOS CFNotificationCenter observer for AppleColorPreferencesChangedNotification, and the current file carries a complete MacAccentWatcher (struct at line 113, install_mac_accent_watcher subscribing to the distributed notification) alongside the Windows watcher; the module doc reads 'Live OS accent-color watcher (Windows + macOS)'. Code follows REQ-08060 (a-wins) on both platforms, so REQ-16221's 'partial / macOS deferred' status is stale and could drive duplicate work or a false 'still TODO' status rollup for a shipped feature.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/platform/accent_watcher.rs:113` @ `74199796c5`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16221, REQ-08060
- **Fix:** Update REQ-16221 to status 'implemented' and drop the 'macOS equivalent is deferred' clause: MacAccentWatcher (AppleColorPreferencesChangedNotification observer) is present in platform/accent_watcher.rs and emits SYSTEM_ACCENT_CHANGED on macOS just like the Windows watcher.

##### `p2-m-i18n-002` · p2-m-i18n · _verification_

**The CI i18n check TOLERATES/RESETS all drift and is structurally incapable of surfacing real missing keys, because sort:true churns ~2200 insertion-ordered keys into a multi-thousand-line diff that buries the net +70 signal.**

i18next.config.ts sets sort:true (line 24), but the committed locale files are hand-maintained in insertion order (en top-level: common@2, app@7, nav@11, build@32 — not alphabetical). So every extract produces a full-file reorder (diff = 5395 added / 5237 removed lines across the two files) in which the 70 genuinely-changed keys are invisible. The config comment (line 12) claims the check 'stays a clean no-op when the locales are in sync' — unattainable given sort:true over unsorted files. The quality.yml i18n job compounds this: it only runs on PRs (if github.event_name != 'push', line 173), is continue-on-error (line 190), and after any diff it emits a ::warning:: then 'git checkout -- packages/translations/src/locales' (line 198) to RESET the drift. Net: the drift never lands, but the completeness gate mandated by REQ-16064/REQ-11044 is de-facto non-functional, which is why Finding 001 shipped.

- **Evidence:** `packages/translations/i18next.config.ts:24` @ `977cea621f` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:2` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `.github/workflows/quality.yml:198` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-06050, REQ-16064
- **Fix:** Either commit a one-time sorted normalization of the en/de locale files (making sort:true a true no-op), or set sort:false. Then make the ADDED-key subset of the extract diff a blocking check (diff only new keys, ignore reorders) so REQ-16064's gate actually fires instead of being buried and restored.

##### `p2-m-i18n-003` · p2-m-i18n · _verification_

**~43 of the 70 'missing' keys extract wants to add (support.diagnostics._, support.recovery._ and siblings) are referenced only by orphaned, never-mounted components, so the extractor's premise 'added key = untranslated UI' is false and dead keys persist — violating REQ-09020.**

The /support route renders only SupportPage (routes/support.tsx line 5), which renders FAQ accordions from support.faq.* (SupportPage line 22). Nine diagnostic tab components — ScrapingDiagnostics, RecoveryTools, AIRuntimeDiagnostics, SystemHealthDashboard, PerformanceMemory, LogsDiagnostics, KnowledgeBase, ContactFeedback, DocumentTroubleshooting — are imported in 0 files (orphaned). ScrapingDiagnostics (line 89) references support.diagnostics.captchaIssue etc.; committed support.diagnostics (en line 2358) only holds provider*/lastScrape* keys, and the real strings live under support.faq.diagnostics.* — so extract reports the dead paths as MISSING. Because removeUnusedKeys:false, whole dead subtrees also remain (support.systemHealth en line 2151, plus support.aiRuntime/performance/logs/knowledgeBase/contact/documentParsing). Both the phantom adds and the retained dead keys violate REQ-09020 ('only add keys for UI that actually renders; remove dead keys'), and they inflate the diff that hides Finding 001.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/ScrapingDiagnostics/index.tsx:89` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/SupportPage/index.tsx:22` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/support.tsx:5` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:2151` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-09020, REQ-16064
- **Fix:** Delete the nine orphaned support diagnostic components and their dead locale subtrees (support.systemHealth/aiRuntime/performance/logs/knowledgeBase/contact/documentParsing plus the non-FAQ support.diagnostics/recovery keys). The live Support page needs only support.faq.*. This clears the phantom missing-key noise so the check's remaining diff is real.

##### `p2-m-js-001` · p2-m-js · _verification_

**The verify pipeline's i18n:extract step silently rewrites the committed en/de translation.json files and there is no --check gate, so locale drift is unverifiable.**

verify/i18n-extract.log shows `i18next-cli extract` ran and 'Updated' both packages/translations/src/locales/en|de/translation.json; verify/i18n-extract.post shows both files as git-modified (' M'), and verify/tree-drift-i18n-extract.diff is ~500KB (5397 insertions / 5239 deletions). The committed locale files are not in the extractor's canonical (alphabetically sorted) form (committed starts common/app/nav; fresh extraction starts '${base}', ai, aiGenerate...), so re-extraction always produces massive reorder churn AND the extractor emits a bogus '${base}': { 'details': '${base}.details' } key from an unresolvable dynamic t(`${base}.details`) call. Because the step runs plain `i18n:extract`(translations/package.json line 17) with no companion`i18n:check`/`--check`— unlike the IPC codegen which is gated by`tsx scripts/gen-ipc-rust.ts --check` (verify/gen-ipc-check.log) — genuine missing-key drift (a dev adds t('newKey') but forgets the de/en value) is buried in the reorder noise and never fails CI. This is exactly the failure mode REQ-03040 shipped as a user-facing bug (contact settings showed raw i18n keys).

- **Evidence:** `packages/translations/package.json:17` @ `9fd01065ef` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:1` @ `3e85c34180`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03040
- **Fix:** Add an `i18n:check` script (run extract then `git diff --exit-code` on the locale files, or i18next-cli's CI/verify mode) and wire it into pre-push + CI alongside gen:ipc:check; normalize the committed en/de translation.json to the extractor's canonical sorted output so real missing-key drift becomes detectable instead of hidden in reorder churn; and replace the dynamic t(`${base}.details`) call site with static keys the extractor can resolve.

##### `prompts-001` · prompts · _consistency_

**The small/medium-ollama resume-generation system prompt hardcodes English section headers ('Professional Summary · Work Experience · Education · Skills') and a US date example, contradicting the locale-driven requirement and its own localized user prompt.**

REQ-03028 (PR #192, commit b2396100) made @ajh/prompts 'locale-driven' and REQ-16117 states ALL prompt market behaviour keys off the job-ad locale, 'not a fixed US/German style'. The resume generator's full and task system-prompt paths honor this (they defer to 'the target market's standard headers ... the task provides the exact headers', resume.ts:130/98), the entire analyze module reconciles the tension explicitly in all three tiers (marketNote: 'Treat these and their direct English equivalents as ATS-standard ... not a fixed US or German style', analysis-prompt.ts:74-76), and even the newer Resume Builder brief path was written correctly ('use the localized headers the task provides', builder-prompt.ts:65). Only the resume generator's BRIEF branch (buildResumeSystemPrompt, the ollama small/medium path) was baked at that same #192 commit with hardcoded 'REQUIRED SECTION HEADERS (exact spelling): Professional Summary · Work Experience · Education · Skills' (resume.ts:59-61) plus a US date example 'January 2021 – March 2023' (resume.ts:63), and was never reconciled. The SAME builder's user prompt demands the localized headers (conventionsNote 'use these section headers — {conv.headers...}', resume.ts:264) and the localized output skeleton emits e.g. PROFIL/BERUFSERFAHRUNG for a German job (resume.ts:354-365). So a German/French/etc. job on a small local model receives two directly conflicting instructions — a rigid 'exact spelling' English list in the system prompt vs. localized headers in the user prompt — and the weakest models (the exact tier this brief path targets) are the most likely to obey the hard system directive and emit English headers on a non-English resume, inconsistent with the localized body.

- **Evidence:** `packages/prompts/src/generate/resume/resume.ts:60` @ `b23961009e` · `packages/prompts/src/generate/resume/resume.ts:264` @ `b23961009e`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03028, REQ-16117, REQ-16118
- **Fix:** In buildResumeSystemPrompt's brief branch, replace the hardcoded 'REQUIRED SECTION HEADERS (exact spelling): Professional Summary · Work Experience · Education · Skills' and the US date example with the same defer-to-task wording the Resume Builder brief path and the analyze marketNote already use (e.g. 'use the localized section headers the task provides, consistently; one consistent date format — the task gives an example'), so the small/medium-ollama path matches the locale-driven full/task paths and its own localized user prompt.

##### `renderer-core-a-001` · renderer-core-a · _traceability_

**Global search via Cmd/Ctrl+K was never wired: the sidebar 'Search' entry was removed 'in favor of Cmd/Ctrl+K', but no Cmd/Ctrl+K handler exists anywhere and only dead command-palette scaffolding remains.**

REQ-04037 (PR #239) asked for a keyboard-shortcut set that explicitly included 'Cmd/Ctrl+K search'. REQ-04038 (PR #252/#254) and REQ-05020 (PR #252) then removed the sidebar 'Search' nav entry and the onboarding tour's search step 'in favor of Cmd/Ctrl+K'. All three are ledgered status=implemented, but the Cmd/Ctrl+K replacement is absent from current code. useKeyboardShortcuts handles only g-prefixed route jumps, '?', and Cmd/Ctrl+',' (no 'k' branch); the ShortcutsOverlay cheat-sheet lists no Cmd/Ctrl+K; there is no search route under routes/; the Sidebar NAV_SECTIONS has no Search item; the Rust shell registers only CmdOrCtrl+1..7/,/R menu accelerators (no Cmd/Ctrl+K) and never emits SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE (the const in ipc_contracts/events.rs is defined but has zero emit sites); and the renderer's useCommandPaletteShortcut hook (a remnant of the command palette deliberately removed by REQ-01068/PR #112) has zero consumers (grep across the repo matches only its own definition). Net effect: global search was dropped from the app and its promised keyboard replacement was never implemented, while the palette IPC event + client namespace + renderer hook survive as dead end-to-end scaffolding. Per-page filter inputs (Applications/Documents) still exist, which limits severity, but there is no global search or quick-nav surface as the owner intended.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/components/layout/Sidebar/index.tsx:37` @ `2d953096da` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts:42` @ `89c131e730` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/services/use-support/use-support.ts:76` @ `a7e6cb5c5d`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04038, REQ-05020, REQ-04037, REQ-01068
- **Fix:** Decide one direction. If global search is still wanted: register a Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut in the shell that emits SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE, and mount useCommandPaletteShortcut on an actual search/quick-nav modal (or add a 'k' branch to useKeyboardShortcuts), and add the row to the ShortcutsOverlay cheat-sheet. If global search is intentionally abandoned: delete the dead scaffolding (useCommandPaletteShortcut in services/use-support/use-support.ts, the onCommandPalette mock stub in lib/mock-client, the shortcuts.onCommandPalette client namespace + contract, and the SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE const/event) and correct the UX-audit ledger to record that Search was removed without a replacement.

##### `renderer-feat-1-001` · renderer-feat-1 · _drift_

**The autopilot 'Keyword Coverage %' relabel promised by ADR-020 / ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md was never surfaced in the UI: autopilot and the Jobs page render the identical unlabeled High/Medium/Low MatchBand pill.**

REQ-16230 / ADR-020 (adr-020-unified-autopilot-scoring-kernel.md:22) asked to unify autopilot ranking on documents::keywords::coverage_score AND to 'Rename the Autopilot metric in UI/analytics as "Keyword Coverage %", clearly distinct from "Match %" (the combined Jobs metric)'. docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:159 marks this DONE (a green check) with the exact text 'relabeled "Keyword Coverage %" to distinguish from Jobs "Match %"'. The scoring-unification half shipped (commands/autopilot.rs build_found_job uses coverage_score), but the relabel half did not: AutopilotCard renders `<MatchBand value={job.score} variant="coverage" />` and the Jobs page renders `<MatchBand value={score.combined} />`, and MatchBand (lib/match-band.tsx) shows only a tier word via t(`jobs.matchBand.${band.key}`) => 'High'/'Medium'/'Low'. The `coverage` variant changes only the internal cut points (55/30 vs 75/50) and colors, never the displayed text, and adds no %, tooltip, or aria label. Net: two fundamentally different scores (keyword-coverage vs combined semantic+ATS) are shown as visually identical pills, so the user-facing distinction the ADR required does not exist, while the status doc claims it is delivered.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/match-band.tsx:56` @ `65bf53014c` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/autopilot/components/AutopilotCard/index.tsx:442` @ `65bf53014c`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16230
- **Fix:** Either surface the distinguishing label the ADR requires (e.g. an aria-label / tooltip / adjacent caption on the coverage-variant MatchBand reading 'Keyword Coverage', mirroring the Jobs page score-guidance affordance), or, if tier pills are intentionally label-less, correct docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:159 and ADR-020 to stop claiming a UI relabel that isn't there.

##### `renderer-feat-3-002` · renderer-feat-3 · _orphans_

**The entire support diagnostics/health/recovery/knowledge-base UI subtree (~22 components) has been dead since the initial release — scaffolded and exported but never wired to any route.**

features/support/components contains a full diagnostics suite: SystemHealthDashboard (+HealthCard/MemoryBar/ProviderCard/MetricCard/OptimizationCard), KnowledgeBase (+KBArticleCard), RecoveryTools (+RecoveryAction), LogsDiagnostics (+LogEntry/LogCategoryCard), ScrapingDiagnostics, PerformanceMemory, AIRuntimeDiagnostics, DocumentTroubleshooting (+DocumentIssueCard/IssueCard/DiagnosticItem). None are imported anywhere outside features/support (verified by whole-repo git grep); the /support route renders only the FAQ SupportPage, and this has been true since v0 (e2d4438: the route rendered only the FAQ, the barrel re-exported these components but nothing consumed them). REQ-16477 (marked 'implemented', no bound commits) asked for 'health checks (Ollama connectivity, DB integrity) and reset tools (clear cache, reimport documents, factory reset) under Settings -> Support' — that UI exists here as unmounted components (only a factory-reset lives elsewhere, in Settings>Privacy), and REQ-08041's 'telemetry-free monitoring UI' was instead delivered by the separate, live features/monitoring route, leaving this subtree as superseded scaffolding.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/SystemHealthDashboard/index.tsx:3` @ `e2d4438489` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/support.tsx:5` @ `e2d4438489`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16477, REQ-08041
- **Fix:** Decide the direction and reconcile: either wire the intended health-checks + recovery/reset tools under /support (fulfilling REQ-16477) reusing these components, or delete the orphaned subtree now that features/monitoring covers live monitoring — carrying ~22 unmounted components indefinitely is dead weight and misleads the ledger's 'implemented' status.

##### `rust-ai-b-001` · rust-ai-b · _orphans_

**AutopilotTarget.top_n and Autopilot.total_applied are dead auto-apply-era config still persisted and serialized, with top_n's doc comment ('how many top-scoring postings autopilot should apply to after scraping') directly contradicting the standing never-apply guard.**

Commit 7e7542cb (PR #241) removed the browser-automation auto-apply engine and repositioned autopilot as a find-and-notify apply assistant; the standing guard (REQ-16014/REQ-16208) says autopilot finds, ranks, and notifies — never applies. Yet AutopilotTarget still carries top_n (defaulted to 3, serialized as topN to disk and the renderer) whose doc still says it controls how many postings autopilot should 'apply to'; nothing in the run path (autopilot_scrape, autopilot_run, the notes loop) ever reads it, and the TS contracts (packages/shared) contain no topN at all, so it is permanently 3. Likewise total_applied is hardwired: record_run is always called with 0 (commands/autopilot.rs:309) and overwrites any prior value, yet the field remains persisted and exposed. The AI-notes top-N (ASSISTANT_NOTES_MAX) is a separate constant, so top_n is not repurposed. AutopilotStore::load's own comment (autopilot/mod.rs:501-505) documents the silent migration off the removed action/autoSubmit fields but left these two siblings behind.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/autopilot/mod.rs:105` @ `7e7542cbed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/autopilot/mod.rs:249` @ `7e7542cbed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/autopilot.rs:309` @ `7e7542cbed`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16014, REQ-16208
- **Fix:** Drop top_n (and its default_top_n helper) from AutopilotTarget and remove total_applied from Autopilot/record_run (or at minimum rewrite top_n's doc and stop serializing both), relying on serde's ignore-unknown-fields for legacy records exactly as was done for the removed action/autoSubmit keys.

##### `rust-data-001` · rust-data · _traceability_ — _arbiter: downgrade (was higher)_

**Factory reset never deletes the persisted Chromium login profiles under browser-state/<board_id>/, so authenticated board session data survives the 'fresh-install state' wipe.**

REQ-04008 (PR #205, commit 4a871de0) required privacy_reset_app to return the app to a fresh-install state, and README (REQ-16469) promises a wipe 'back to a clean install'. The reset command only calls board_login::disconnect per board, which writes auth-status=false and removes cookies.json (scraping/board_login/mod.rs:264-268, comment: 'more reliable than nuking the profile directory'). The Chromium user-data dir browser-state/<board_id>/profile — created by the CDP login window and containing the browser's own authenticated cookie DB / localStorage — is never removed anywhere (the only remove_dir_all in the crate is in platform/linux_appimage.rs). After a factory reset, re-opening the login window reuses that profile and can silently resume the old authenticated session; personal auth data persists on disk after a GDPR-style reset. Not even a best-effort removal (like the conversations.db boot cleanup in lib.rs:662-670) is attempted.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/board_login/mod.rs:152` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/src/locales/en/translation.json:805` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04008, REQ-16469, REQ-01067, REQ-04018
- **Fix:** In privacy_reset_app, after disconnecting each board, best-effort std::fs::remove_dir_all the browser-state/<board_id> directories (retry/ignore Windows file-lock errors, or schedule deletion on next boot like the conversations.db cleanup).
- **Arbiter note:** Facts hold at HEAD: privacy_reset_app only disconnects+wipes registry stores (privacy.rs:215-223); disconnect keeps profile (mod.rs:264-268); Chromium user-data-dir browser-state/<id>/profile created (mod.rs:152) never deleted (only remove_dir_all is a test, linux_appimage.rs:343). Real privacy remanence, but stores ARE wiped, residue is local + inert until explicit re-login → medium not high.

##### `rust-data-002` · rust-data · _orphans_

**Core docs still describe the deleted Node-era data layer — a single app.db plus a LanceDB vector store — which has not existed since PR #69 moved everything to per-domain SQLite files.**

REQ-01009 (PR #69, commit f50b99a6, 2026-05-21) moved documents/embeddings/vector search into native Rust SQLite and deleted the NeDB+LanceDB sidecar store; REQ-03065 records the decision that embeddings live in SQLite 'rather than a separate vector engine'. Yet docs/DEVELOPMENT.md:237-245 tells developers the data lives in app.db with a LanceDB vectors/ dir and to delete both to reset dev state; docs/DEPLOYMENT.md:326-327 shows the same layout; docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:378 has a 'LanceDB Collections' section; docs/PATTERNS.md:269-308 documents an import pipeline through Tesseract OCR, Ollama embedding and 'Upsert to LanceDB'. None of app.db, vectors/, LanceDB, or Tesseract exist in the Rust code — real files are documents.db, ai_generations.db, applications.db, job_preferences.db, contact_profile.db, referrals.db, jobs.db, pipeline_cache.db. The reset instructions are actively wrong (deleting app.db does nothing). REQ-16223's own doc warning ('re-audit the matrix, hybrid search etc.') was never completed for these sections.

- **Evidence:** `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md:243` @ `f50b99a6ac` · `docs/DEPLOYMENT.md:327` @ `f50b99a6ac` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:378` @ `f50b99a6ac` · `docs/PATTERNS.md:283` @ `f50b99a6ac`
- **Requirements:** REQ-01009, REQ-03065, REQ-16490, REQ-12040, REQ-16223
- **Fix:** Rewrite the storage sections of DEVELOPMENT.md/DEPLOYMENT.md/ARCHITECTURE.md/PATTERNS.md to the current per-domain SQLite layout (list the real .db files; embeddings in documents.db; extraction via pdf-extract/quick-xml, no OCR/LanceDB/Ollama-only pipeline).

##### `rust-data-007` · rust-data · _drift_

**InteractionStore is the one bundle-participating store that never got the uniform DataStore trait — commands/data.rs carries a bespoke export_all/import_bundle branch for it, contrary to the 'uniform trait across every persistent store' requirement.**

REQ-02039 (PR #145, commit e22cc29d) and the standing architecture claim REQ-16212 require 'a uniform DataStore trait (key/export/import with REPLACE semantics) across every persistent store', with commands/data.rs assembling the bundle generically. Seven stores implement the trait (documents, ai_generations, applications, job_preferences, contact_profile, referrals, autopilot), but InteractionStore is special-cased in both build_bundle (data.rs:112-115, hardcoded 'interactions' key + json!(export_all())) and data_import (data.rs:247-263, manual serde deserialize + import_bundle). Behavior is equivalent today, but the asked-for uniformity was silently left incomplete: any future generic handling of DataStore implementors (e.g. iterating a registry) would silently skip interactions, and the key literal is duplicated instead of coming from key().

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/data.rs:112` @ `e22cc29de9` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/data.rs:247` @ `e22cc29de9`
- **Requirements:** REQ-02039, REQ-16212, REQ-03068
- **Fix:** Implement DataStore for Mutex<InteractionStore> (key='interactions', export -> export_all, import -> validate + import_bundle) and route it through the same import_into/build_bundle path as the other stores.

##### `rust-docs-embed-001` · rust-docs-embed · _orphans_

**The `match_resume_batch` IPC command — documented (ADR-017/API.md) as the live scoring path — is registered but never invoked by the app; the renderer scores per-row on demand via the single `match_resume`.**

Phase K / ADR-017 (REQ-16011, REQ-08040, REQ-16714) shipped `match_resume_batch` to 'score all postings in one Rust pass' with a MATCH_BATCH_MAX=1000 DoS guard, distributed per-job by MatchScoresProvider. The current renderer instead gates a SINGLE-match query: `MatchScoresProvider` → `useRowMatchScore` → `useJobMatchScore` → `api.match.resume` (the single `match_resume` command), fired lazily only when a row is opened. `api.match.resumeBatch` exists as a client binding (namespaces/match/match.ts:7) and a mock noop, but has ZERO production callers anywhere in apps/desktop/src; the agent tool also calls single `match_resume`. So the batch command, its `MatchResumeBatchRequest` contract, `MATCH_BATCH_MAX`, and the `job_texts_for` batch helper are dead-but-wired. The architecture docs are now inaccurate: ADR-017:109 and API.md:719 present the batch pass as the scoring path (API.md:719 even points to a nonexistent `renderer/providers/match-scores-provider.tsx`; the real file is `features/jobs/providers/MatchScoresProvider.tsx`).

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/match_resume.rs:322` @ `81f6c70cc8` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:828` @ `81f6c70cc8`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16011, REQ-08040, REQ-16714, REQ-16224, REQ-16223
- **Fix:** Either delete `match_resume_batch` + `MatchResumeBatchRequest` + `MATCH_BATCH_MAX` + `job_texts_for` + the client binding/mock (renderer already scores per-row on demand), or re-wire MatchScoresProvider to use it; then correct ADR-017:109 and API.md:711-719 (including the stale provider path) to describe the actual on-demand single-match scoring path.

##### `rust-platform-001` · rust-platform · _drift_

**The anti-abuse-limits knowledge doc describes a fictional limiter API and falsely claims the caps are runtime-configurable via system_set_performance_mode, when they are fixed compile-time constants nothing reconfigures.**

REQ-16019 (sourced from docs/knowledge/anti-abuse-limits.md) records the command-boundary limiter as implemented INCLUDING 'make all limits runtime-configurable via system_set_performance_mode'. The real limiter (limits/mod.rs, introduced by PR #381/REQ-08057, ai_research bucket retrofitted by PR #550/REQ-15019) exposes FIXED `pub const` tuning values (AI_GENERATE_RATE_MAX=20, AI_GENERATE_CONCURRENCY_MAX=3, PROVIDER_DAILY_MAX=2000) and a `Limiter{ per_command: Mutex<HashMap>, per_provider_day: Mutex<HashMap> }` with `acquire()` + `charge_provider_daily()`. system_set_performance_mode (commands/system/mod.rs) only pushes concurrency->ScraperEngine and keep_alive/cache knobs->performance::set(); it NEVER touches the limiter, and limits/mod.rs itself lists 'a settings UI to configure the caps (today they are fixed constants)' as an intentionally out-of-scope follow-up. So the doc's line 99 'All are runtime-configurable via system_set_performance_mode' is false and that sub-ask is silently unimplemented. Beyond that, the doc is wholesale fictional: it documents a nonexistent `RateLimiter{ per_second_semaphore: Arc<Semaphore>, daily_usage: Arc<DashMap<String, DailyBucket>> }` struct, `state.limits.check_ai_generate(...)`/`check_scrape(...)` methods (the real API is `acquire`/`charge_provider_daily`), and a config table (max_concurrent 8, per_second 10, daily 100k/50k) whose every number disagrees with the real constants. Because docs/knowledge is a first-class agent context source (CLAUDE.md context-priority), this actively misleads future work about a security-relevant subsystem and violates the repo's thin-pointer/no-drift rule.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/limits/mod.rs:46` @ `7da5c92f27` · `docs/knowledge/anti-abuse-limits.md:99` @ `74199796c5` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/system/mod.rs:191` @ `7da5c92f27`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16019, REQ-08057, REQ-15019
- **Fix:** Rewrite docs/knowledge/anti-abuse-limits.md as a thin pointer to limits/mod.rs (real Limiter + acquire()/charge_provider_daily() API and the actual constants incl. the ai_research + agent_run buckets), delete the fictional RateLimiter struct/check_* methods/config-table, and either implement runtime-configurable caps in system_set_performance_mode or drop the claim and record fixed-constant caps as an explicit non-goal.

##### `scraping-a-001` · scraping-a · _traceability_

**Berlin Startup Jobs title-split regex still only handles the ' at ' separator, never the '//' form the project's own live-feed recon confirmed is the primary title format.**

REQ-10039 (board reconnaissance, docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md introduced in PR #461 / commit 5002ff9f) recorded that Berlin Startup Jobs feed titles are primarily 'Job Title // Company Name' (also 'Title at Company') and explicitly recommended changing the repo regex from ' at (.+)$' to r'(?:\s+at\s+|//)(.+)$' to handle both separators 'confirmed in live feed'. That change never landed: berlinstartupjobs/mod.rs still compiles AT_COMPANY_RE = ' at (.+)$' (line 9) and derives clean_title via title.split(" at ") (line 76). For a '//'-delimited title the regex misses, so company is set to 'Unknown' and clean_title keeps the whole '... // Company' string — wrong user-facing company + title data on a board that is still registered and guest-scrapable (boards/mod.rs SCRAPERS). The ledger itself flagged this ask as unverified/undelivered.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/berlinstartupjobs/mod.rs:9` @ `5002ff9fac` · `docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md:355` @ `5002ff9fac`
- **Requirements:** REQ-10039
- **Fix:** Update AT_COMPANY_RE to r'(?:\s+at\s+|//)(.+)$' and derive clean_title by splitting on the matched separator (not a hardcoded ' at '), so '//'-delimited Berlin titles extract company and title correctly; add a fixture test for the 'Title // Company' form.

##### `scraping-b-001` · scraping-b · _consistency_

**Berlin Startup Jobs title-split regex still only handles ' at ', not the live-verified 'Title // Company' separator the endpoint reconnaissance told the team to support.**

The 2026-06-20 board reconnaissance (PR #461, commit 5002ff9f) live-verified that the BSJ RSS feed titles come in BOTH formats — 'Job Title at Company' and 'Job Title // Company' — and recorded an explicit recommendation to update the repo regex from ' at (.+)$' to '(?:\s+at\s+|//)(.+)$' (docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md 'Recommendation' section, still present at HEAD). The ledger (REQ-10039) already noted no implementing commit was found; audit confirms the current scraper still compiles AT_COMPANY_RE = r" at (.+)$" and falls back to company="Unknown" with the '// Company' suffix left embedded in the title for every feed item using the '//' separator.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/berlinstartupjobs/mod.rs:9` @ `5002ff9fac` · `docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md:355` @ `5002ff9fac`
- **Requirements:** REQ-10039, REQ-11043
- **Fix:** Update AT_COMPANY_RE to r"(?:\s+at\s+|\s*//\s*)(.+)$" (and split the clean title on the same pattern instead of the hardcoded " at "), with a fixture test covering both separators; or explicitly record in SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md that the recommendation was rejected.

##### `scraping-b-002` · scraping-b · _consistency_

**The explicitly requested 15-minute posted-date filter was never implemented — the finest preset is 30m in both the TS schema and the Rust codegen mirror, and no commit ever contained a 15m option.**

On 2026-06-21 the owner asked for finer posted-date options: 'past 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours, 4 hours, and 8 hours' (REQ-12022). The delivering PR #470 (commit ca59779f) shipped DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS = ['30m','1h','2h','4h','8h','24h','week','month'] — 15m was silently dropped, not removed later: git grep shows no '15m' token at PR #470's merge, at PR #473's parent, or at HEAD. PR #473 (commit 99e7286d) then centralized the same 30m-finest set into the Rust codegen mirror (ipc_contracts/date_filters.rs) with a Rust exhaustiveness test, cementing the gap. The ledger marks the ask 'partial' with medium confidence ('appears not to have survived'); audit confirms it never existed.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/date_filters.rs:5` @ `ca59779f66` · `packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts:135` @ `99e7286d3a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-12022
- **Fix:** Either add '15m' to DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS in packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts, run pnpm gen:ipc, and map it in adzuna_max_days_old/jsearch_date_posted/apify_f_tpr (Adzuna's max_days_old is whole-days, so 15m would clamp to 1 day server-side — document that), or get the owner to formally retire the 15-minute ask.

##### `scraping-b-003` · scraping-b · _orphans_

**Apply-era 'applied' counters survive the ordered auto-apply removal: Autopilot.total_applied is persisted and IPC-exposed but hardcoded to 0 at its only call site, and the AutopilotRun interface (with applied/skipped counters) has no consumer at all.**

REQ-03026 (PR #190) made autopilot runs fetch-and-save only, and REQ-04039 (PR #241, commit 7e7542cb) ordered the auto-apply surface deleted end to end including the 'Applied counter'; REQ-16014 records a standing guard to never reintroduce it. Yet the Autopilot struct still carries total_applied (autopilot/mod.rs:249), record_run still takes a total_applied parameter, and the sole production caller passes a literal 0 (commands/autopilot.rs:309) — so the field is dead weight serialized into every persisted autopilot and across IPC (shared type Autopilot.totalApplied, types/index.ts:250). The shared AutopilotRun interface with found/matched/applied/skipped counters (types/index.ts:297-307) is referenced by nothing but its own declaration — a fully dead type from the apply-loop era.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/autopilot.rs:309` @ `7e7542cbed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/autopilot/mod.rs:249` @ `6717a2f175` · `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts:304` @ `7e7542cbed`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04039, REQ-03026, REQ-16014
- **Fix:** Drop total_applied from the Autopilot struct/record_run signature (serde(default) keeps old JSON loading), remove totalApplied from the shared schema via gen:ipc, and delete the unused AutopilotRun interface.

##### `tauri-shell-001` · tauri-shell · _consistency_

**The shipped CSP now permits third-party Clearbit egress, contradicting the still-current security-rules.md baseline that egress is limited to Ollama and that loosening the CSP is HIGH/CRITICAL.**

REQ-03057 / REQ-10023: the security authority's own doc (docs/knowledge/security-rules.md:10) states 'keep the policy tight; local AI egress is limited to Ollama (127.0.0.1:11434). Loosening CSP is HIGH/CRITICAL,' and PR #435 (44aa3472) deliberately narrowed connect-src to the exact loopback ports with ZERO external hosts to reconcile with that decision. Later, the opt-in company-logo feature RE-loosened the CSP by adding 'https://autocomplete.clearbit.com' to connect-src and 'https://logo.clearbit.com' to img-src (tauri.conf.json:30). The feature is default-off and UI-disclosed, and use-company-logo.ts labels it a 'security-reviewed exception,' but security-rules.md:10 was never updated and no ADR or lesson records the exception (a repo-wide grep of docs + lessons.jsonl returns zero Clearbit references). Result: the documented security invariant that tauri-security-reviewer relies on now contradicts shipped config — a future reviewer either flags the CSP as HIGH/CRITICAL per the doc, or trusts the doc and misses that egress is no longer Ollama-only. The company name of every browsed posting is sent to Clearbit/HubSpot when the toggle is on, so this is a real (if opt-in) departure from the local-first egress posture.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:30` @ `44aa3472fa` · `docs/knowledge/security-rules.md:10` @ `f7f0efac82`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03057, REQ-10023
- **Fix:** Record the Clearbit egress as an explicit ADR/exception and amend security-rules.md:10 to name the two allowed opt-in logo hosts, OR route the logo fetch through the Rust net::http shared() client and drop the connect-src grant entirely (the hook's own documented TODO). Either way reconcile the doc so the CSP loosening is no longer un-memorialized.

##### `tauri-shell-002` · tauri-shell · _consistency_

**The bundle still builds, publishes, and advertises a Windows MSI installer, contradicting ADR-021's decision that per-machine MSI scope is no longer supported and cannot auto-update.**

ADR-021 (commit 74199796, PR #381, REQ-08057) pinned the Windows installer to NSIS currentUser scope, stating explicitly that 'The auto-updater only applies to NSIS, not MSI,' that legacy per-machine MSI installs 'could not receive updates' and drift out of sync (stale exe post-update), and that 'per-machine scope ... is no longer supported' — legacy MSI users must clean-uninstall+reinstall. Yet the current bundle.targets still list 'msi' (tauri.conf.json:38), and release.yml builds it, uploads apps/desktop/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/msi/*.msi to every GitHub release (release.yml:420), and advertises a first-class 'MSI' download link in the auto-generated release notes (release.yml:747) — with no 'no auto-update' caveat, even though the same workflow comment (release.yml:414-416) acknowledges MSI produces no .sig and is not updater-consumed. A new user who picks the advertised MSI lands in exactly the un-updatable per-machine state ADR-021 set out to eliminate. The msi target is a leftover of the pre-ADR-021 dual NSIS+MSI build direction (REQ-00045) that ADR-021 superseded but never removed from config/release.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:38` @ `74199796c5` · `.github/workflows/release.yml:747` @ `74199796c5`
- **Requirements:** REQ-08057, REQ-08042
- **Fix:** Drop 'msi' from bundle.targets and stop uploading/advertising the MSI so NSIS currentUser is the sole Windows installer, OR — if MSI must remain for offline/enterprise installs — explicitly label it 'manual updates only' in the release notes and ADR-021 so it stops silently re-exposing the drift ADR-021 fixed.

##### `testing-001` · testing · _orphans_

**The machine-enforced architecture test still classifies two non-existent modules (`layout`, `measure`) in its L1 layer array, orphans of the printpdf layout engine deleted in the Typst migration.**

REQ-04031 (#228, commit 60c00de) migrated all PDF export to Typst and 'delete[d] the entire printpdf stack' including the top-level `layout` module (git show confirms apps/tauri/src-tauri/src/layout/mod.rs existed at 60c00de^ and was gone at 60c00de). `measure` likewise no longer exists as a module (no file/dir under src, zero `crate::measure` references). Yet tests/architecture.rs still lists both in the L1 constant (lines 50-51), and docs/architecture-rules.md still lists them (line 26 / L1 contract line 55). The arch test only guards that EXISTING modules are classified (every_module_is_classified) and only has dead-entry guards for R2_ALLOW and R7_ALLOW — there is NO reverse guard that every name in the L0-L3 arrays maps to a real module. So these two dead entries rot silently: the 'cannot rot / dead-entry-guarded' discipline the owner explicitly requested for allowlists (REQ-04003) was never extended to the module inventory. No runtime break (the suite is green), but the enforced contract misrepresents the crate's module set.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs:50` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs:51` @ `60c00dea1b` · `docs/architecture-rules.md:26` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04031, REQ-04003
- **Fix:** Delete `layout` and `measure` from the L1 arrays in both tests/architecture.rs (lines 50-51) and docs/architecture-rules.md (line 26 and the L1 contract at line 55). Add a reverse guard asserting every name in L0-L3 corresponds to an existing top-level module — the same dead-entry discipline already applied to R2_ALLOW/R7_ALLOW — so the inventory can no longer rot.

##### `testing-003` · testing · _consistency_

**R8's documented 1100-LOC hard cap with per-file 'capped at current size; may not grow' enforcement contradicts the enforced test, which applies a single global 1400-LOC cap with no per-file guard — five modules currently exceed the documented cap while passing the real gate.**

REQ-04003 asked that 'oversized W-6 files are capped' and that R8 be a real test/gate. docs/architecture-rules.md R8 (line 136) states 'No source module > 1100 LOC (warn > 600)' with 'the 10 W-6 files, capped at current size; may not grow'. The enforced test instead sets HARD_CAP_LOC = 1400 and SOFT_LOC = 600 with NO per-file allowlist (architecture.rs line 509), so any W-6 file can silently grow to 1400 — the 'may not grow' per-file guarantee is not implemented at all. Five non-test modules currently exceed the documented 1100 cap while passing the actual 1400 gate: scraping/boards/aggregator/mod.rs (1234), autopilot_helpers/mod.rs (1182), scraping/scrape_url/mod.rs (1161), agent/controller.rs (1126), documents/mod.rs (1115). The test's R8 rationale comment (line 509) still cites 'export/pdf_renderer/mod.rs (~1343)' as the 'current ceiling', but that file was reorganized into export/pdf/ by the Typst migration and no longer exists (current max non-test file is aggregator/mod.rs at 1234). Both the documented threshold and the stated enforcement mechanism diverge from what the gate does.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs:509` @ `a6face0339` · `docs/architecture-rules.md:136` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04003
- **Fix:** Reconcile R8: either lower HARD_CAP_LOC to the documented number and split the 5 offenders, or update the doc to the real 1400 cap. Drop the unimplemented 'per-file capped at current size; may not grow' claim, or implement a per-file baseline guard so W-6 files genuinely cannot grow. Refresh the stale 'export/pdf_renderer/mod.rs (~1343)' comment at architecture.rs:509 to the actual current ceiling.

##### `ui-package-001` · ui-package · _drift_

**docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md still declares the accent 'violet' throughout, but the shipped default accent has been the teal→peach→coral sweep (#5bb8b7) since PR #383 — the canonical design doc contradicts the shipped default.**

REQ-09005 (2026-06-14/15) explicitly replaced the violet default accent with the teal-peach-coral three-stop sweep, shipped in commit 22298c3d (PR #383, v0.104.0): --color-brand went from #a855f7 (violet) to #5bb8b7 (teal), verified via git show against the parent commit. Yet DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — the doc snapshot regenerated LATER (2026-06-24, REQ-13020) and treated as the design-system source of truth for agents — still states 'Accent stays violet (--color-brand), not Apple's Action Blue' (line 9) and repeats the violet premise at lines 24, 32, 132, 182, 236. tokens.css carries the same stale comments next to the teal values: line 156-161 says 'violet stays the primary brand identity' / '(violet)' while line 166 correctly documents the teal→peach→coral sweep two lines below. Agents and future design work consuming DESIGN_SYSTEM.md inherit a false premise about the default accent.

- **Evidence:** `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md:9` @ `22298c3d7a` · `packages/ui/src/css/tokens.css:161` @ `22298c3d7a` · `packages/ui/src/css/tokens.css:48` @ `22298c3d7a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-09005, REQ-13020
- **Fix:** Sweep DESIGN_SYSTEM.md (lines 9, 24, 32, 132, 182, 236) and the tokens.css comments at lines 156-161 to describe the current teal→peach→coral default accent (violet remains only as a preset/history note); route via project-steward docs sync.

##### `ui-package-002` · ui-package · _consistency_

**The ProgressBar percentage label uses text-foreground/45 at a sub-14px size, below the design system's own documented WCAG AA opacity floor (text-foreground/70 minimum for <14px text; '<50% fails'), leaving the REQ-10027 contrast fix half-done.**

The 2026-06-19 fleet audit (REQ-10027) flagged the ProgressBar percentage label for WCAG AA contrast and asked to raise it; PR #439 (commit 91d86694) was the implementing sweep, and the ledger itself notes this LOW sub-finding was 'presumed included ... but unconfirmed'. Current code has the label at 'text-[10px] text-foreground/45' (ProgressBar.tsx:52). DESIGN_SYSTEM.md line 86 — the standing rule from the same design system — mandates 'text-foreground/70 as the minimum floor' for text under 14px and states '<50% fails' 4.5:1 on light backgrounds. 45% is above the audit's original 20% but still below the documented floor, so the label still fails the design system's AA rule in light mode. (The 10px size itself is lifted to 12px by the utilities.css sub-12px floor, so only the opacity is non-compliant.)

- **Evidence:** `packages/ui/src/components/ProgressBar/ProgressBar.tsx:52` @ `91d8669467` · `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md:86` @ `91d8669467`
- **Requirements:** REQ-10027
- **Fix:** Raise the ProgressBar label to text-foreground/70 (or a per-scheme token) to meet the documented <14px opacity floor; verify against both schemes.

#### ⚪ Low (75)

##### `ci-release-002` · ci-release · _consistency_

**A comment in release.yml's update-download-page job claims the no-`[skip ci]` push 'also fires' the 'push-only `release` job', but release.yml has been workflow_dispatch-only (no push trigger) since PR #418 — the release job cannot fire on push.**

The update-download-page job deliberately omits `[skip ci]` so its commit re-triggers pages.yml (which watches landing/**). Its comment (release.yml:848-849) adds 'The push-only `release` job also fires but no-ops (a lone chore commit yields no semantic-release version bump).' That parenthetical was accurate when the comment was written (PR #384, 2026-06-16, when the release workflow still ran semantic-release on push to main), but REQ-10015 / PR #418 (commit f54b3f4f, 2026-06-18) gated the whole release behind `workflow_dispatch` + `action: release` and removed the push trigger. The same file now states at its header (release.yml:8) 'workflow_dispatch only — nothing runs automatically on push to main', directly contradicting the line-848 claim. Harmless at runtime (the pages.yml re-trigger — the real intent — still works), but the stale claim is a residual of the exact push-vs-manual release drift REQ-16404 flagged and REQ-10015 was meant to close, and could mislead a maintainer into thinking pushes to main trigger releases.

- **Evidence:** `.github/workflows/release.yml:848` @ `f54b3f4f3b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-10015, REQ-09041
- **Fix:** Delete or correct the 'push-only `release` job also fires but no-ops' sentence in release.yml's update-download-page comment; the push only re-triggers pages.yml now. Keep the accurate first half (omit `[skip ci]` to redeploy the landing site).

##### `export-a-003` · export-a · _drift_

**EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md still claims 'typst-svg is a dev-dependency, never shipped' and that 'engine.rs and render.rs are the only files that import the typst and typst_pdf crates', but since the SVG live-preview work typst-svg is a runtime dependency used by engine.rs, and the actual typst importers are engine.rs + world.rs (render.rs imports none).**

PR #331 (commit 3a3f1c37, REQ-07003/REQ-12029/REQ-16696) moved the AI-Generate preview to production SVG rendering (render_resume_svg_pages / render_letter_svg_pages), which required promoting typst-svg from dev-dependency to [dependencies] — Cargo.toml even carries the note '(typst-svg moved to [dependencies] — it now backs the live-preview render path.)'. The normative doc's cover-letter-previews section (line 202) still asserts the pre-#331 hard-wall ('typst-svg is a dev-dependency, never shipped'), directly contradicting the doc's own Live-preview section a few lines later, and the isolation-boundary section (line 53) names the wrong file set (render.rs contains no typst imports; world.rs is a heavy importer; engine.rs also imports typst_svg). The ring-fence itself still holds in spirit — all typst types stay inside typst_engine/ and public signatures are typst-free — so this is contract-text drift, not a boundary breach.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml:153` @ `3a3f1c37b5` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:202` @ `3a3f1c37b5` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/engine.rs:210` @ `3a3f1c37b5` · `docs/EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md:53` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16695, REQ-16687, REQ-12029, REQ-07023
- **Fix:** Update EXPORT_TEMPLATES.md: isolation boundary = 'engine.rs and world.rs import typst/typst_pdf/typst_svg' and delete/qualify the 'typst-svg is a dev-dependency, never shipped' sentence (it now ships to back the live preview).

##### `export-a-004` · export-a · _drift_

**README still advertises the printpdf-era glyph-subsetting result ('fonts are glyph-subsetted per export… shrinking a typical résumé PDF from ~3 MB to ~120 KB'), but that subsetter (allsorts, PR #206) was deleted with the whole printpdf stack in the Typst migration and the figures were never re-measured against the Typst engine.**

REQ-04010 (PR #206, commit 1feb72a3) implemented allsorts glyph subsetting with the ~3 MB → ~120 KB outcome and a size-regression test; the ledger marks it superseded by REQ-04031 (PR #228 removed pdf_renderer/fonts.rs and the printpdf font pipeline). Typst's PDF emitter does subset embedded fonts, so the qualitative claim stays roughly true by accident, but the README feature bullet (and ledger entry REQ-16460, still marked 'implemented' citing README.md:112) traces to a deleted implementation and pre-migration numbers with a 4-family/11-face bundle now shipping instead of the printpdf-era set.

- **Evidence:** `README.md:112` @ `1feb72a3dc` · `README.md:112` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04010, REQ-16460, REQ-04031
- **Fix:** Re-measure a typical export with the Typst engine and update or soften the README bullet (e.g. 'compact PDFs — Typst embeds only subsetted fonts'), dropping the printpdf-era numbers if they no longer hold.

##### `export-a-005` · export-a · _drift_

**theme/mod.rs's module doc still describes the pre-Typst strangler-fig world — 'the legacy PDF/DOCX renderers import [export::templates] directly' and Template data 'migrates physically into this module… in Phase 2' — a dual-path ghost: the legacy renderers were deleted and the promised migration never happened (nor is planned).**

The theme seam was introduced in Phase 1b (REQ-03007, PR #173) with a comment explaining it would physically absorb export::templates when the backends moved onto DocumentModel. The Typst migration (REQ-04031, commit 60c00dea) instead deleted the legacy printpdf renderers outright and left Template in export/templates/, consumed by both the Typst engine and model_docx via the theme functions (placement_for / is_two_column / link_style are all live). The comment now asserts the existence of renderers that are gone and a migration phase that was superseded, which is exactly the kind of dual-path remnant the migration was supposed to leave none of. Code-comment drift only; all theme functions are in active use.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/theme/mod.rs:9` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/theme/mod.rs:5` @ `7a3447e7f2`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03007, REQ-04031
- **Fix:** Rewrite the theme/mod.rs module doc to the current reality: theme is the canonical seam for placement/two-column/link-style decisions consumed by the Typst engine and model_docx; drop the Phase-1/Phase-2 narrative and the legacy-renderer references.

##### `export-b-003` · export-b · _orphans_

**JetBrains Mono and Playfair Display TTFs are orphans of removed premium templates: not bundled by world.rs and referenced by no template or code, yet download-fonts.ps1 still fetches and verifies them.**

The Typst migration (REQ-04031) shipped nine templates; several premium variants were later removed, their ids preserved only as graceful-fallback strings in export/types.rs (lines 54-55 / 217-220 enumerate the removed 'refined-executive', 'editorial-serif', 'mono-technical', etc.). world.rs (comment at line 63) notes 'removed templates' fonts are not bundled here' and indeed bundles only Carlito/Inter/Source Serif 4/Manrope. But apps/desktop/src-tauri/fonts/ still carries jetbrains_mono_{regular,bold}.ttf and playfair_display_{regular,bold}.ttf (the fonts the removed mono-technical / editorial-serif templates used), and download-fonts.ps1 still downloads them (lines 110-123) and lists them in its post-download verification set (lines 134-135). A grep of all src and .typ templates finds zero references to JetBrains or Playfair. They are pure orphans kept alive by the download script — repo bloat plus a maintenance signal that these fonts are still 'required'.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/fonts/download-fonts.ps1:116` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/world.rs:63` @ `60c00dea1b` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/types.rs:54` @ `60c00dea1b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04031, REQ-16457
- **Fix:** Delete the unused jetbrains_mono__.ttf and playfair_display__.ttf from fonts/, and remove their download+verification blocks from download-fonts.ps1, so the on-disk font set matches the four families world.rs actually bundles.

##### `extension-001` · extension · _traceability_

**The extension-import rate limiter guards only the network-fetch branches of handle_import, not the primary captured-DOM parse branch, so a successful DOM-first import counts against no rate/concurrency budget.**

The owner asked to 'add a rate limiter for browser-extension job imports' (REQ-14012, PR #507) and the extension domain standard records the invariant that the unified import 'tries DOM capture first and falls back to URL-only, sharing the scrape_url rate-limiter slot' (REQ-16023). PR #507 turned out to be docs-only (commit 951a4807 'docs: correct extension import mode ... note rate budget'), so REQ-14012 was satisfied by relying on the pre-existing limiter wiring rather than adding a new one. But in the current handle_import (extension_bridge/mod.rs) the limiter slot (acquire_slot) is acquired ONLY on the three network-fetch branches: canonical-resolve, URL-mode resolve, and the unusable-parse fetch fallback. The `else if let Some(h) = html.as_deref()` branch that runs when the extension supplied the authenticated DOM — the DEFAULT path since REQ-10002 (PR #418) made 'always capture the rendered authenticated page DOM' primary — calls parse_from_html with no acquire_slot, and the fallback fetch (guarded by `canonical.is_none() && html.is_some()` AND parse-unusable) never fires for a usable parse. Net: an import that parses successfully from the captured DOM never counts against SCRAPE_RATE_MAX/SCRAPE_CONCURRENCY_MAX, so the documented 'shares the scrape_url rate-limiter slot' claim overstates coverage. Impact is bounded (import is a per-click, user-driven action; the DOM parse does no external network I/O so there is no board-abuse/SSRF vector; dedup by normalized URL prevents unbounded rows) — the only unbounded effect is repeated local upserts + notification pushes on rapid manual re-imports of the same page.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/extension_bridge/mod.rs:667` @ `951a480726` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/extension_bridge/mod.rs:646` @ `f54b3f4f3b`
- **Requirements:** REQ-14012, REQ-16023, REQ-10002
- **Fix:** Either acquire a limiter slot for every import path (wrap the whole posting-resolution block, incl. the parse_from_html branch, in one acquire_slot guard) so the behavior matches the documented 'shares the scrape_url rate-limiter slot' invariant, or narrow the domain-doc/standard wording to state that only the network-fetch import paths are rate-limited (the local DOM parse is intentionally exempt).

##### `ipc-a-001` · ipc-a · _drift_ — _arbiter: downgrade (was higher)_

**credentials.available() returns an object {available:bool} from Rust while the contract, mock, and consumer all expect a bare boolean, so the 'no OS encryption' warning's `=== false` gate is dead in the packaged app.**

REQ-05017 (PR #249, commit 658a45b2) removed the board-credential CRUD and deliberately kept only an availability probe whose stated job is to 'gate the encryption-availability warning' (credentials.ts contract comment). The TS contract types it `available(): Promise<boolean>`, the mock returns bare `false` (mock-client.ts:178) and its test asserts `.toBe(false)`, and the consumer gates the error alert with a strict `encAvail === false` (AccountsSettingsTab/index.tsx:20). But the Rust command returns `json!({ "available": guard.is_available() })` — an OBJECT, not a bare bool (commands/credentials.rs:15) — and the tauri-client passes it through untyped with no unwrap (`available: () => invoke('credentials_available')`, tauri-client/namespaces/credentials/credentials.ts:4). In the packaged Tauri app `encAvail` is therefore `{available:false}`, and `{available:false} === false` is always false, so the `settings.accounts.noEncryptionWarning` error alert NEVER renders on a system where the OS secret store is genuinely unavailable. The bug is invisible to CI: gen:ipc:check does not cover this hand-written command (it returns serde_json::Value, not a generated struct), the tauri-client uses untyped invoke() laundered through an `as AppClient['credentials']` cast (tauri-client/index.ts:65), and every test runs against the mock which returns the correct bare boolean — so mock and real diverge (violating the mock-mirrors-real rule REQ-16041).

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/credentials/mod.rs:90` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-05017, REQ-16041
- **Fix:** Make the seam return the contract shape: either unwrap in the tauri-client (`available: () => invoke<{available:boolean}>('credentials_available').then(r => r.available)`) or change the Rust command to return a bare bool (`credentials_available -> bool`). Add a test that exercises the real return shape (not just the mock) so mock/real cannot drift again.
- **Arbiter note:** Type drift is real at HEAD: rs:15 returns {available:bool}, contract credentials.ts:5 is bare bool, consumer index.tsx:20 uses ===false, seam credentials.ts:4 no unwrap, mock diverges. But is_available() is hardcoded true (credentials/mod.rs:90-92, sole def, test asserts true), so backend never emits false; the warning-when-unavailable scenario is unreachable and the real app correctly renders no warning. Latent landmine, no current impact — not high.

##### `ipc-a-002` · ipc-a · _drift_

**docs/knowledge/event-system.md is stale: it lists Phase 4 (typed Rust payload structs) as 'Planned / currently serde_json::Value fallbacks' though the typed structs are generated and shipped, and it miscounts the event namespaces (says 11, lists 10, actually 12).**

REQ-08004 (PRs #366-368, commit 5d6f4ee) shipped the event centralization including phase 4 'codegen'd typed payloads for ai:stream and jobs:event from zod schemas' and the phase-6 jobs collapse. The generated file apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/event_payloads.rs (header '@generated by pnpm gen:ipc') defines fully typed `AiStreamChunk`/`AiStreamChunkError` and a typed `JobEvent` — not serde_json::Value fallbacks. Yet event-system.md:63-66 still lists Phase 4 and Phase 6 under 'Planned phases' with 'currently serde_json::Value fallbacks', which misstates implementation status for a doc that agents route on (docs/knowledge is a primary context source per CLAUDE.md). Separately, event-system.md:9 says 'all 11 namespaces' and enumerates only 10 (ai, applications, autopilot, boards, jobs, menu, notifications, scrape, shortcuts, updater), while EVENT_CHANNELS (events/index.ts:34-47) actually registers 12, omitting `agent` (agent:step) and `system` (system:accentChanged) from the list. This is the REQ-16022 doc claim ('not yet implemented per the doc') contradicting the REQ-08004/REQ-16021 implemented reality; the code side is correct.

- **Evidence:** `docs/knowledge/event-system.md:65` @ `5d6f4ee143` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/event_payloads.rs:9` @ `5d6f4ee143` · `docs/knowledge/event-system.md:9` @ `5d6f4ee143`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16022, REQ-08004, REQ-16021
- **Fix:** Update event-system.md: move Phase 4 (typed payload structs — event_payloads.rs) to an 'Implemented' note, reconcile the Phase 6 jobs-collapse status, and correct the registry line to 12 namespaces including `agent` and `system`.

##### `ipc-a-003` · ipc-a · _drift_

**docs/ARCHITECTURE.md hardcodes '23 IPC namespaces' but the current IpcContract defines 29 (and IPC_CHANNELS 27).**

ARCHITECTURE.md states '23 namespaces' in the architecture diagram (line 25) and '23 typed namespace definitions' (line 127). The current IpcContract interface in packages/shared/src/ipc/contracts/index.ts enumerates 29 namespace members (agent, aiGenerations, applications, system, jobs, ai, documents, jobPreferences, contactProfile, github, extensionBridge, scrape, match, geocode, credentials, linkedin, boards, cliAgents, privacy, referrals, resume, support, autopilot, menu, notifications, updater, shortcuts, dialog, data). The literal drifted as later features added namespaces (e.g. `agent` via REQ-15027/PR #555 commit 0fbd7c82, `applications`, `github`, `referrals`, `cliAgents`). The ledger note for REQ-16112 explicitly flagged this ('Namespace count is a doc literal - verify against contracts.ts, not the doc'). ARCHITECTURE.md:151 also labels the re-export file `contracts.ts` whereas the re-exports actually live in `contracts/index.ts`.

- **Evidence:** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:25` @ `0fbd7c8294` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:127` @ `0fbd7c8294` · `packages/shared/src/ipc/contracts/index.ts:51` @ `0fbd7c8294`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16112
- **Fix:** Update the '23 namespaces' literal in ARCHITECTURE.md to the current count (or replace the hardcoded number with a pointer), and fix the `contracts.ts` reference to `contracts/index.ts`.

##### `ipc-b-001` · ipc-b · _orphans_

**The `shortcuts` / command-palette IPC seam is dead on both ends — no Rust emitter and no renderer consumer — a leftover of the Cmd/Ctrl+K command palette removed in REQ-09034.**

REQ-00005 introduced the `onCommandPalette` shortcut channel in the (Electron-era) contract. REQ-09034 (commit 22298c3d) then 'removed the orphaned hybrid-search feature ... the Cmd/Ctrl+K shortcut + ShortcutsOverlay entry', deleting the command palette that shortcut opened. The IPC seam survived intact but dead on BOTH ends: Rust defines `SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE` (ipc_contracts/events.rs:20) but never emits it and registers no global shortcut (the global-shortcut plugin is built at lib.rs:493 but `.register()` is never called); the renderer defines `useCommandPaletteShortcut` (use-support.ts:76) but nothing calls it (git grep repo-wide finds only the definition). The full plumbing is still maintained — contract `shortcuts.onCommandPalette`, tauri-client `shortcuts` namespace listener, `EVENT_CHANNELS.shortcuts.commandPalette`, the `shortcut:command-palette` AppEvents entry + lock test, and the mock-client stub — and the shortcuts.ts contract doc comment falsely asserts 'the shell pushes the `shortcut:command-palette` event and the renderer subscribes', which is untrue in current code.

- **Evidence:** `packages/shared/src/ipc/contracts/shortcuts.ts:10` @ `22298c3d7a` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/services/use-support/use-support.ts:76` @ `22298c3d7a` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/events.rs:20` @ `22298c3d7a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-09034, REQ-00005, REQ-08004
- **Fix:** Remove the shortcuts namespace end-to-end (contract, tauri-client namespace, EVENT_CHANNELS.shortcuts + the `shortcut:command-palette` AppEvents/lock-test entry, Rust `SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE`, `useCommandPaletteShortcut`, mock stub, and the landing docs row). If a command palette is actually intended, wire a global-shortcut registration that emits the event and a real consumer that calls the hook; either way, correct the misleading doc comment.

##### `p2-b1-ipc-chain-001` · p2-b1-ipc-chain · _boundary_ — _arbiter: downgrade (was higher)_

**The three Settings > Support copy-to-clipboard buttons invoke Rust commands that do not exist and are not registered, silently regressing implemented REQ-00040.**

The support contract + client declare copyEnvironmentDetails/copyAppVersion/copySystemInfo, which invoke 'support_copy_environment_details' / 'support_copy_app_version' / 'support_copy_system_info'. None of these exist in commands/support.rs (which defines only support_export_diagnostics + a support_get_system_info stub) and none are registered in the lib.rs generate_handler! block. At runtime each call rejects with a Tauri 'command not found' error. In ContactFeedback the handler does `await copyEnvDetails.mutateAsync(); notify.success('Copied to clipboard.')` with no try/catch, so the reject throws before the success toast — the button silently copies nothing with no user feedback (unhandled rejection). REQ-00040 explicitly implemented these buttons to copy via tauri-plugin-clipboard-manager because 'they were no-ops'; the current Tauri code has regressed them back to no-ops. gen:ipc:check cannot catch this because codegen only emits request structs, never verifies that a client-invoked command name is registered.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/support.tsx:5` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/SupportPage/index.tsx:40` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-00040
- **Fix:** Implement + register support_copy_environment_details / support_copy_app_version / support_copy_system_info in commands/support.rs (write to the clipboard via tauri-plugin-clipboard-manager as REQ-00040 specified) and add all three to the generate_handler! list in lib.rs. Alternatively, if copy should be renderer-side, change the client to use the clipboard-manager JS API instead of invoking non-existent commands. Add a build-time check that every client-invoked command name appears in generate_handler.
- **Arbiter note:** Technical facts hold: support.ts:19-21 invokes 3 commands absent from src-tauri; lib.rs:845-846 registers only export_diagnostics + get_system_info stub; ContactFeedback index.tsx:143-176 has no try/catch. But ContactFeedback has ZERO importers repo-wide and route support.tsx:5 renders FAQ-only SupportPage. Buttons are unrendered dead code -> no user-facing impact. Latent, not critical.

##### `p2-b1-ipc-chain-002` · p2-b1-ipc-chain · _boundary_ — _arbiter: downgrade (was higher)_

**The ten Support recovery/reset actions invoke unregistered Rust commands and, because the RecoveryAction handler awaits a void-wrapped mutation, report FALSE success for destructive operations that never run.**

RecoveryTools wires ten actions (rebuildVectorIndexes, clearEmbeddingsCache, resetVectorDatabase, reloadAiRuntime, unloadAllModels, resetModelConfiguration, clearOcrCache, reindexAllDocuments, resetAllSessions, clearScrapingQueue) to client methods that invoke 'support_rebuild_vector_indexes' etc. — commands that do not exist in commands/support.rs and are absent from the lib.rs generate_handler! block, so every invoke rejects with 'command not found'. Worse than a plain error: RecoveryTools passes `onAction={() => void rebuildIndexes.mutateAsync()}`, so the arrow returns undefined; RecoveryAction.run() then does `await onAction()` (awaits undefined → resolves) and unconditionally calls `notify.success(successMessage)`. The mutation rejection is swallowed by the `void` operator. Result: a user troubleshooting a broken app clicks 'Reset Vector Database' / 'Reset All Sessions' (destructive, behind a confirm modal) and sees a green 'Vector database reset.' / 'All sessions reset.' toast while nothing actually executes. The seam mismatch (missing commands) plus the void-swallowed rejection turns a whole recovery panel into confident no-ops. Codegen cannot catch either half.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/support/components/SupportPage/index.tsx:22` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-11007
- **Fix:** Implement + register the ten support recovery commands in commands/support.rs and lib.rs (or gate the panel until they exist). Independently, stop the false-success: have RecoveryTools pass the mutation directly (onAction={() => rebuildIndexes.mutateAsync()}) instead of `void`-wrapping it, so RecoveryAction.run() awaits the real promise and its try/catch surfaces the error toast rather than a fake success.
- **Arbiter note:** Both facts hold: 10 commands absent from src-tauri/src (support.rs registers only support_export_diagnostics:189 + get_system_info; lib.rs:845-846); void-swallow real (RecoveryAction/index.tsx:33-34). But RecoveryTools is orphaned dead code, zero refs in apps/desktop/src outside its own file; SupportPage/index.tsx:22 renders only FAQ accordions. Unreachable, no user impact.

##### `p2-b1-ipc-chain-003` · p2-b1-ipc-chain · _boundary_

**Three Rust IPC commands are registered in generate_handler! but have no contract method and no client invoke anywhere — orphaned command surface left behind by refactors.**

lib.rs registers commands::resume::extract_resume (line 800), commands::boards::boards_list (line 837), and commands::privacy::privacy_clear_data (line 840). None is invoked by any tauri-client namespace, renderer, or extension, and none has a corresponding contract method: the resume contract exposes only extractText → resume_extract_text (a different command, in match_resume.rs), the boards contract has catalog/connect/disconnect/getStatus/importCookies but no list(), and the privacy contract has only signOutAll/clearInteractions/resetApp. These are dead IPC entry points — the Rust command surface drifted ahead of the contract when the renderer paths were renamed/consolidated. Harmless today but they are unnecessary, always-callable IPC surface and a maintenance/audit trap.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:800` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:837` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:840` @ `04e6e78aed`
- **Fix:** Remove extract_resume, boards_list, and privacy_clear_data from generate_handler! and delete their now-unused command functions (verify no future feature needs them first). Consider a CI check flagging registered commands with no client invoke.

##### `p2-b2-shared-schemas-002` · p2-b2-shared-schemas · _boundary_

**`RuntimeHealth.workers` is a required Electron-era JobQueue field that the Tauri `system_health` producer never emits, so `health.workers` is permanently undefined — an unsatisfiable required-field contract and a latent crash for any consumer that dereferences it.**

`RuntimeHealth` (packages/shared/src/types/index.ts) declares `workers: { active; idle; max }` as a REQUIRED field — the worker pool of the pre-Tauri TS `JobQueue` (REQ-00024's 'JobQueue telemetry'). After Electron/JobQueue removal (53a331170), the Rust `system_health` producer returns `{scraper, ai, cliAgents, data}` and never includes `workers` (it also returns an undeclared `scraper` field, the mirror asymmetry). No renderer code reads `health.workers` today, so the breakage is latent rather than active — but the declared contract is impossible to satisfy, and any consumer that later does `health.workers.active` will throw on undefined. Same Electron->Tauri drift class as finding 001; the `RuntimeHealth` type was never trimmed to the Rust shape.

- **Evidence:** `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts:129` @ `53a3311706` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/system/mod.rs:28` @ `d5da71f2f9`
- **Requirements:** REQ-00024
- **Fix:** Drop `workers` from `RuntimeHealth` (JobQueue no longer exists) and add the `scraper: { mode; ready; scrapers }` field the Rust `system_health` actually returns, so the type matches the producer both ways.

##### `p2-b3-extension-protocol-001` · p2-b3-extension-protocol · _boundary_

**The extension manifest's Firefox-id comment contradicts the Rust origin allowlist it cites: it says the gecko id mirrors an entry in auth.rs::ALLOWED_EXTENSION_IDS and that the Chrome id is 'still assigned at publish', but auth.rs has no Firefox entry and the Chrome id is already pinned.**

manifest.ts's FIREFOX_EXTENSION_ID comment states the gecko id 'Mirrors the Firefox entry in auth.rs::ALLOWED_EXTENSION_IDS exactly. (The Chrome CWS id is still assigned at publish)'. Both halves are false in current code. (1) auth.rs::ALLOWED_EXTENSION_IDS holds ONLY the pinned Chrome id 'oaoekkgkhmgdfnpmfkpphgiikliaicll'; auth.rs itself documents the gecko id is 'intentionally absent from this list' (Firefox is admitted by moz-extension UUID shape + null Origin, never a pinned id), and its unit test rejects_firefox_gecko_id asserts exactly that.
