The gecko id's actual mirror is register.rs::FIREFOX_GECKO_ID (native-messaging allowed_extensions) plus the extension's own browser_specific_settings.gecko.id. (2) The Chrome id is no longer 'assigned at publish' — it is pinned, as the SAME file's header (line 18: 'The published Chrome Web Store id IS pinned in auth.rs::ALLOWED_EXTENSION_IDS') and apps/extension/README.md line 273 both state, so line 30 self-contradicts line 18. The header sentence (lines 12-13) further overgeneralizes that pairing works only when 'chrome-extension://<id> / moz-extension://<id>' is 'in that list', which is untrue for Firefox. The constant VALUES are all correct (job-importer@aijobhunter.app matches register.rs; the Chrome id matches auth.rs+register.rs), so there is no runtime/behavior impact — only the comment drifted after the Chrome-id pin (REQ-08053) and the Firefox-UUID-shape acceptance (REQ-08032). Concrete risk: a maintainer trusting this comment could add the gecko id to ALLOWED_EXTENSION_IDS (breaking the rejects_firefox_gecko_id invariant) or believe the Chrome id is not yet pinned and skip re-pinning on republish.

- **Evidence:** `apps/extension/src/manifest.ts:30` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/extension_bridge/auth.rs:18` @ `04e6e78aed`
- **Requirements:** REQ-08053, REQ-08032
- **Fix:** Rewrite the FIREFOX_EXTENSION_ID comment in manifest.ts: say the gecko id mirrors register.rs::FIREFOX_GECKO_ID (the native-messaging allowed_extensions entry) and the extension's browser_specific_settings.gecko.id — NOT auth.rs::ALLOWED_EXTENSION_IDS, where Firefox is intentionally absent and admitted by moz-extension UUID shape / null Origin. Delete the stale '(The Chrome CWS id is still assigned at publish)' clause since the id is pinned, and reword the header (lines 12-13) so it no longer implies a Firefox origin appears 'in that list'.

##### `p2-b4-prompts-providers-001` · p2-b4-prompts-providers · _boundary_

**Provider-aware native structured-output metadata is built and advertised in @ajh/prompts but is never consumed by the app and never honored by the Rust provider adapters.**

REQ-03028/PR #192 made @ajh/prompts provider-aware including 'structured-output metadata': resolveProfile() sets structuredOutput=true for cloud, structuredOutputFor() returns the concrete OpenAI response_format / Anthropic forced-tool-use / Gemini responseSchema spec, and the package README advertises cloud structured output as 'yes (structuredOutputFor)'. But structuredOutputFor has ZERO callers in apps/desktop (only the package's own tests + dist reference it), the renderer never threads any schema/structured spec over IPC, and no Rust cloud adapter (openai.rs / anthropic.rs / gemini.rs) emits a response_format json_schema, Gemini responseSchema, or Anthropic forced-tool-use for JSON — the only tool_choice in the cloud path is 'auto' for the agent tool loop. The renderer even flags the deferral (buildProviderProfile: 'the Rust adapter wave will wire this'), but that wave never landed. Net effect: cloud analysis/metadata extraction silently relies on the same validate-and-repair fallback as ollama; the provider-aware structured-output layer is dead flexibility across the seam and the README overclaims a capability that is not wired.

- **Evidence:** `packages/prompts/src/provider/index.ts:285` @ `b23961009e` · `packages/prompts/README.md:29` @ `b23961009e`
- **Requirements:** REQ-03028, REQ-16116
- **Fix:** Either wire the structuredOutputFor spec through IPC and honor it in the cloud Rust adapters (OpenAI response_format json_schema, Anthropic forced tool-use, Gemini responseSchema), or, if the native-structured-output wave is abandoned, delete structuredOutputFor plus the structuredOutput/supportsStructuredOutput fields and drop the README's 'yes (structuredOutputFor)' claim so the package stops advertising an unwired capability.

##### `p2-b4-prompts-providers-002` · p2-b4-prompts-providers · _boundary_

**PR #563 humanized the TS interview-questions builder and the Rust agent resume/cover-letter prompts but left the Rust agent interview-questions prompt with no natural-voice or anti-AI-tell guidance.**

REQ-15039/PR #563 asked to humanize all AI-generated application text — explicitly including interview questions — 'plus compact humanization lines in the Rust agent prompts'. In the single commit 169f7ae8, packages/prompts/.../interview-questions.ts gained ANTI_AI_TELL_PROSE + HUMANIZE_PROSE, and tools.rs's RESUME_SYSTEM and COVER_LETTER_SYSTEM gained inline humanization (cadence/specificity/bullet-variety), but INTERVIEW_QUESTIONS_SYSTEM in the SAME tools.rs received neither the anti-AI-tell bans nor any humanization line. suggest_interview_questions is a live tool in prep_application_tools() (step 6 of the PREP_APPLICATION flow), so within one 'Prep this application' run the drafted resume and cover letter carry natural-voice guidance while the interview questions do not — a silent partial that can leave the AI-tell vocabulary (leverage/robust/delve, promotional adjectives) the renderer's interview-questions path would strip. Impact is modest (short 'Q:' lines, honesty spine intact) but the seam is genuinely one-sided.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/agent/tools.rs:308` @ `169f7ae8e3` · `packages/prompts/src/generate/interview-questions/interview-questions.ts:83` @ `169f7ae8e3` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/agent/tools.rs:278` @ `169f7ae8e3`
- **Requirements:** REQ-15039
- **Fix:** Add the compact anti-AI-tell + humanization line to INTERVIEW_QUESTIONS_SYSTEM in tools.rs (mirroring the RESUME_SYSTEM/COVER_LETTER_SYSTEM treatment from #563), or, if interview questions are intentionally excluded from backend humanization, add a short code comment stating so, so the gap reads as a decision rather than an oversight.

##### `p2-b5-translations-seam-004` · p2-b5-translations-seam · _boundary_

**Fully-variable dynamic t() bases materialize as literal phantom keys ('${base}', 'jobs.trust.flags.${flag}') in the extractor output — benign at runtime but permanent advisory noise and a resource-pollution risk.**

PerformancePreferences builds `const base = ...` then calls t(`${base}.details`) / t(`${base}.label`) etc., and trust-badge.tsx calls t(`jobs.trust.flags.${flag}`). The static extractor cannot resolve a leading variable, so it emits literal keys `"${base}": { "details": "${base}.details" }` and `"jobs.trust.flags.${flag}"` (present as additions in the recorded extract diff for both en and de). At runtime these families are fully covered — every concrete key exists (settings.performanceMode.backend/options.*, and jobs.trust.flags has exactly the 4 keys matching the Rust TrustFlag enum missingApplyUrl/invalidUrl/suspiciousDomain/companyDomainMismatch), so there is no missing-translation bug. But the config's documented dynamic-key allowlist (i18next.config.ts:9, lists only models.tier/analyze.groups/aiGenerate.wizard.emphasis) is stale for these patterns, the phantoms add to the perpetually-dirty advisory diff (finding 003), and if a developer runs the documented extract-and-reconcile workflow and commits the output, the '${base}' / '${flag}' literals get written into the locale JSON as junk keys.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/preferences/PerformancePreferences/index.tsx:131` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/trust-badge.tsx:46` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/i18next.config.ts:9` @ `977cea621f`
- **Requirements:** REQ-06050, REQ-09020
- **Fix:** Give these call sites an extractor-visible base (e.g. a literal prefix or i18next-cli's // t(...) hints) so the leading variable is not top-level, or refactor to non-template lookups, so the extractor stops materializing '${...}' literal keys. Refresh the config:9 dynamic-key comment to cover the ${base} / trust-flag families.

##### `p2-b6-test-ids-001` · p2-b6-test-ids · _boundary_

**The template-picker app/test link exists only through a test mock: production hardcodes id="template-picker" instead of TEST_IDS.documents.templatePicker, so the two sides can silently drift.**

PR #471 centralized data-testid literals into @ajh/test-ids specifically 'so test ids cannot drift between app and tests' (REQ-16489), defining documents.templatePicker='template-picker'. The current TailorFlow/GenerationOutput.tsx (created after all three test-ids commits) renders the picker as <Dropdown id="template-picker"> — a hardcoded string literal passed as the id prop, NOT a reference to the constant. The real @ajh/ui Dropdown never emits a data-testid (packages/ui/src/components/Dropdown/Dropdown.tsx uses id only for aria-labelledby and does not spread props), so in production no element carries data-testid="template-picker". The only reason getByTestId(TEST_IDS.documents.templatePicker) resolves is the test's own Dropdown mock (GenerationOutput.test.tsx:98 renders data-testid={id ?? 'dropdown'}). Because the produce side is a decoupled literal, renaming the constant value would break the query while the app keeps emitting the old id — exactly the drift the package was meant to prevent.

- **Evidence:** `packages/test-ids/src/test-ids.ts:93` @ `11981580c5` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/documents/components/TailorFlow/GenerationOutput.tsx:256` @ `11981580c5` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/documents/components/TailorFlow/GenerationOutput.test.tsx:98` @ `11981580c5`
- **Requirements:** REQ-12066, REQ-16489
- **Fix:** Reference the constant on the produce side: id={TEST_IDS.documents.templatePicker} in GenerationOutput.tsx. Better, make the real Dropdown forward its id (or an explicit testId prop) to a data-testid so the assertion exercises production markup instead of a mock, or drop documents.templatePicker from the map if it is only a mock hook.

##### `p2-b6-test-ids-002` · p2-b6-test-ids · _boundary_

**TEST_IDS.documents.saveBtn ('save-btn') is a ghost id: no component (production or test stub) renders it, and its only consumer is a negative assertion that passes vacuously.**

documents.saveBtn was centralized at #471 as a live app data-testid. The later auto-commit rework (TailorFlow/GenerationOutput.tsx did not exist at #471/#476/#479) removed the explicit Save button, but the constant was left in the map. A repo-wide search shows nothing renders data-testid="save-btn"; the sole reference is GenerationOutput.test.tsx:692, expect(screen.queryByTestId(TEST_IDS.documents.saveBtn)).not.toBeInTheDocument(). Because the id exists nowhere, that guard passes regardless of app behavior — it cannot catch a re-introduced Save button unless the button happens to reuse this exact id, so it gives false regression confidence while the constant sits dead in the central map.

- **Evidence:** `packages/test-ids/src/test-ids.ts:91` @ `11981580c5` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/documents/components/TailorFlow/GenerationOutput.test.tsx:692` @ `11981580c5`
- **Requirements:** REQ-12066, REQ-16489
- **Fix:** Delete the documents.saveBtn constant and either remove the vacuous 'no Save button is rendered' assertion or re-anchor it to a real element (e.g. assert the auto-commit/pending-commit indicator is present) so the guard actually verifies the auto-save UX.

##### `p2-b7-events-deeplink-001` · p2-b7-events-deeplink · _boundary_

**The `shortcut:command-palette` event seam is fully orphaned: a registered channel + Rust const + renderer listener + service hook all survive, but nothing emits it and nothing consumes the hook after the command palette feature was removed.**

The command palette was deleted wholesale (REQ-01068: 'Remove the command palette entirely — component, app-store Zustand store, titlebar trigger'), but the entire event-seam scaffolding was left behind and never cleaned up. The renderer still registers a listener for `shortcut:command-palette` (shortcuts.ts:9 via EVENT_CHANNELS.shortcuts.commandPalette) and still exposes `useCommandPaletteShortcut` (use-support.ts:76), yet that hook has ZERO consumers anywhere in apps/desktop/src (git grep at HEAD returns only its definition). On the Rust side the `SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE` const exists (events.rs:20) but is never emitted — the global-shortcut plugin is registered as an empty `Builder::new().build()` with no handler, and use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts:40 explicitly states 'Intentionally NOT a command palette (out of scope)'. The shared lock test even documents this at events.test.ts:87 ('no Rust emit — frontend/global-shortcut only'), but the 'frontend/global-shortcut only' emitter was never implemented on either side. Net: a dead cross-language seam (shared registry entry + AppEvents type + Rust const + client namespace method + service hook) with no emitter and no consumer.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/tauri-client/namespaces/shortcuts/shortcuts.ts:9` @ `7d2d759968` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ipc_contracts/events.rs:20` @ `47111d9a08` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/services/use-support/use-support.ts:76` @ `7d2d759968` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts:40` @ `7d2d759968`
- **Requirements:** REQ-01068, REQ-00005, REQ-04037
- **Fix:** Delete the dead command-palette seam: remove SHORTCUTS_EVENTS.commandPalette from packages/shared/src/events/shortcuts.ts, the `shortcut:command-palette` AppEvents entry (index.ts:73) and its lock-test line, SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE from events.rs, the shortcuts client namespace method, and the unused useCommandPaletteShortcut hook. If a command palette is planned to return, re-add the emitter (a global-shortcut handler emitting SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE) and a consumer at the same time so the seam is never one-sided.

##### `p2-b9-003` · p2-b9-declared-boundaries · _boundary_

**CLAUDE.md declares renderer feature-isolation (rule 9) and the ui/prompts/translations package boundaries (rule 12) as enforced, but eslint.config.mjs implements a boundary block only for packages/shared -- none of those four are eslint-enforced, and prompts-no-window is not TypeScript-enforced either because tsconfig.base.json ships the DOM lib.**

eslint.config.mjs has a package-boundary no-restricted-imports block only for packages/shared (line 413: no React/Node). There is no equivalent block for packages/ui (rule: no Zustand/IPC/routing), packages/prompts (rule: no UI/window), or packages/translations (rule: no app/IPC), and no rule banning cross-feature imports among renderer features/*. CLAUDE.md's header states rules are 'enforced by ESLint, TypeScript, commitlint, and CI'; rule 9 (line 56) says 'Never import across feature dirs' and rule 12 (line 59) lists all four package boundaries. Of these, only the ui/translations 'no app/IPC' half is implicitly covered (their package.json omit those deps), while prompts-no-window is covered by neither eslint (no block) nor TypeScript -- tsconfig.base.json's lib includes DOM/DOM.Iterable, so `window`/`document` typecheck fine inside packages/prompts. There are 0 live violations today (grep found no cross-feature imports and no window/IPC leaks), so this is a latent gap rather than active breakage, but the CLAUDE.md rule table overstates the enforcement the eslint config actually provides.

- **Evidence:** `eslint.config.mjs:413` @ `2f208c12ca` · `CLAUDE.md:59` @ `2f208c12ca`
- **Requirements:** REQ-00006, REQ-16063, REQ-16109
- **Fix:** Either add eslint blocks for packages/ui, packages/prompts (incl. no-restricted-globals on window/document), and packages/translations mirroring the shared block, plus a cross-feature-import ban for apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/*, OR soften CLAUDE.md rules 9/12 to state these are convention-only (not ESLint-enforced). The eslint blocks are preferable since the rule table already reads as enforced.

##### `p2-contra-extension-003` · p2-contra-extension · _contradiction_

**The open decision REQ-16627 (30s auth timeout; bad_token vs distinct auth_timeout) was de facto resolved in code but never recorded, and the ledger note misstates the shipped behavior as 'timeout->bad_token'.**

Since the auth handshake landed (commit 7f8ad372, PR #432, REQ-11004), bridge.ts resolves an auth timeout as {error:'auth timeout'} (line 486) and the handler at lines 511-520 deliberately does NOT enter bad_token for it — it closes and transitions to app_not_running + reconnect, reserving bad_token for the explicit 'unauthorized' reply. That is the opposite of the ledger note's claim that 'conservative timeout->bad_token behavior shipped in the meantime'. The code resolution is coherent, but the decision entry stays 'unclear' and the recorded interim behavior is wrong, so the contradiction map would propagate a false premise.

- **Evidence:** `apps/extension/src/lib/bridge.ts:486` @ `7f8ad372a4` · `apps/extension/src/lib/bridge.ts:511` @ `7f8ad372a4`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16627, REQ-11004, REQ-16626
- **Fix:** Record the decision: auth timeout is treated as a transport failure (reconnect, never bad_token) with the shared 30s REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS; close REQ-16627 or file the distinct auth_timeout phase as an explicit deferral.

##### `p2-contra-ipc-shared-004` · p2-contra-ipc-shared · _contradiction_

**REQ-03085's documented future direction (generate TS bindings FROM Rust via tauri-specta) contradicts the shipped single-source-of-truth decision (Zod TS schemas generate Rust via gen-ipc-rust.ts, REQ-16473) and was never updated or superseded.**

docs/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md still proposes 'Generating TypeScript bindings directly from Rust types (e.g. tauri-specta)' to 'remove the manual contract maintenance'. PR #472 (5299a13c) and the gen:ipc pipeline established the opposite direction: packages/shared Zod schemas are the SSOT and packages/shared/scripts/gen-ipc-rust.ts generates the Rust structs, with CI drift checks (REQ-16473, REQ-12069). Two opposite codegen source-of-truth directions coexist in the record; only the response-typing half of the doc's claim remains valid.

##### `p2-contra-performance-001` · p2-contra-performance · _contradiction_

**The `match_resume_batch` batch-scoring command is a shipped-but-uncalled ghost: batch scoring (REQ-08040) was reversed to on-demand per-job scoring (REQ-13028), yet the batch command, its client wrapper, its tests and its docs all survive as if batch is the live path.**

REQ-08040 (#378, 81f6c70) replaced the per-row scoring crawl with a single batched `match_resume_batch` backend pass (+ `useJobMatchScores` hook, ADR-017) and deleted ScoringScheduler. REQ-13028 (#486, bc6c9e) then reversed the model: it rewrote MatchScoresProvider to on-demand per-job scoring (`scoreJob` fires only when a job is opened, RowMatchScore reads a per-job `useJobMatchScore` query) and JobsResults now does 'no batch-all on mount'. #486 never touched `commands/match_resume.rs`, so `match_resume_batch` (MATCH_BATCH_MAX=1000 DoS cap, ADR-017 cache-sharing) remains a registered Tauri command with a client wrapper (`match.resumeBatch`), a mock, and Rust unit tests — but ZERO callers in the renderer or the Rust core (autopilot included). REQ-16011 and REQ-16714 are both recorded as `implemented` describing this batch pass as THE scoring mechanism ('match.batch scores all postings in one Rust pass ... distributed per-row via MatchScoresProvider/useRowMatchScore') — internally incoherent (batch-all vs on-demand per-row) and not matching the live code. docs/API.md:717 and ADR-017 still document `match.batch` as the active scoring API. Resolution is mixed: frontend follows the newer on-demand ask (REQ-13028 wins) while the batch command + client + docs + ledger records follow the older, reversed ask. No user-facing breakage (on-demand scoring works), but a fully-wired unused IPC surface plus stale 'implemented' requirement records and misleading docs.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/match_resume.rs:322` @ `81f6c70cc8` · `apps/desktop/src/tauri-client/namespaces/match/match.ts:7` @ `81f6c70cc8` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/jobs/providers/MatchScoresProvider.tsx:26` @ `bc6c9e8789`
- **Requirements:** REQ-08040, REQ-13028, REQ-16011, REQ-16714
- **Fix:** Either delete the dead batch path (remove `match_resume_batch` command + lib.rs registration + `match.resumeBatch` client wrapper + mock + tests + API.md/ADR-017 'active' wording), or wire it back to a real caller (e.g. batch-score the visible page on the jobs list) if the batch pass is still wanted. Then mark REQ-08040/REQ-16011/REQ-16714's batch mechanism as superseded by the REQ-13028 on-demand model so the ledger stops recording a reversed design as implemented.

##### `p2-contra-platform-infra-001` · p2-contra-platform-infra · _contradiction_

**The removed native splash subsystem is still asserted as current by unsuperseded ledger requirements and a stale in-repo scratch doc, contradicting the shipped React in-app splash.**

REQ-16635/16636/16638 (all sourced from .claude/scratch/native-splash-screen.md) describe a native theme-aware splash WINDOW, a <data_dir>/ui-theme theme-mirror contract, app_ready/RevealGuard IPC, main window visible:false, and a splash L3 module. That subsystem shipped in #470 (ca59779f, 2026-06-22 01:44) and was explicitly deleted the SAME day by #475 (48f9158f, 20:43, 'replace the native splash window with an in-app overlay' = REQ-12065). Current code follows REQ-12065 and nothing else: main.tsx renders React <AppSplash/> (line 44), tauri.conf.json main window is visible:true, and grep finds no splash module / app_ready / set_theme_mirror / RevealGuard / ui-theme anywhere in apps/desktop. Yet REQ-16635 is still ledgered status 'implemented' with superseded_by null (REQ-16636 'documented', REQ-16638 'unclear'), none pointing at REQ-12065, and the scratch handoff doc remains in the repo describing the removed subsystem using pre-#512 apps/tauri/ paths (also renamed to apps/desktop by REQ-14027). Code resolves the conflict correctly (React overlay wins), so no runtime defect — but the ledger + scratch-doc drift can misdirect an agent into re-adding the splash L3 module (REQ-16638) or maintaining the theme-mirror writer for Rust code that no longer reads it.

- **Evidence:** `.claude/scratch/native-splash-screen.md:6` @ `48f9158f17` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx:44` @ `48f9158f17`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16635, REQ-16636, REQ-16638, REQ-12065
- **Fix:** Mark REQ-16635/16636/16638 superseded_by REQ-12065 in the ledger, and delete (or stamp a 'SUPERSEDED by #475 — native splash replaced by React AppSplash' banner on) .claude/scratch/native-splash-screen.md so no agent reintroduces the native splash window, theme-mirror file, or app_ready IPC that #475 removed.

##### `p2-contra-renderer-core-001` · p2-contra-renderer-core · _contradiction_

**REQ-16636 (theme-mirror file contract) and REQ-16637 (app_ready IPC follow-up) describe the native-splash design that REQ-12065 deleted, yet neither is marked superseded and REQ-16637 sits open as status 'unclear'.**

The native-splash plan (.claude/scratch/native-splash-screen.md -> REQ-16636/REQ-16637) specified a <data_dir>/ui-theme theme-mirror file, written by the renderer on theme apply and read by Rust for splash theming, plus an app_ready command added to SystemContract + SYSTEM_CHANNELS that the renderer calls after first paint. REQ-12065 (PR #475, commit 48f9158f) reversed exactly that: it replaced the native splash window with an in-app React overlay and its message 'remove[s] the app_ready + set_theme_mirror IPC commands + their SystemContract entries and channels' and deletes splash/mod.rs (RevealGuard, reveal paths, theme-mirror) + public/splash.html. Current code confirms the reversal held: components/layout/AppSplash/index.tsx documents the overlay is mounted at the app root with 'no IPC dependency'; packages/shared/src/ipc has no app_ready; and no ui-theme/theme-mirror token exists anywhere in app code (only inside landing/benchmarks/data.js commit-log data). Because REQ-16636 stays 'documented' and REQ-16637 stays 'unclear' with no superseded_by, both still read as open frontend follow-ups for a feature that was removed; acting on REQ-16637 as written would re-introduce the deleted app_ready IPC and theme-mirror write.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/components/layout/AppSplash/index.tsx:19` @ `48f9158f17`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16636, REQ-16637, REQ-12065
- **Fix:** Mark REQ-16636 and REQ-16637 superseded_by REQ-12065 (native splash + app_ready + set_theme_mirror + theme-mirror removed) and delete or annotate .claude/scratch/native-splash-screen.md so the theme-mirror + app_ready plan is not re-implemented against the now-IPC-free in-app AppSplash.

##### `p2-contra-scraping-002` · p2-contra-scraping · _contradiction_

**The user-requested 15-minute posted-date filter option (REQ-12022) is absent from the centralized DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS, whose finest preset is 30m.**

REQ-12022 explicitly asked for a 'past 15 minutes' option alongside 30m/1h/2h/4h/8h. When date-filter presets were centralized in PR #473 (commit 99e7286d, REQ-12063), DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS became ['30m','1h','2h','4h','8h','24h','week','month'] — the 15m option was silently dropped. REQ-12022 was never marked superseded or abandoned (status 'partial'), and the ledger itself flagged '15-minute option appears not to have survived'. Because DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS is the Zod enum validating dateFilter, a '15m' value is now rejected end-to-end. This may be an intentional simplification (15-min-old postings are rare), but the drop is undocumented and contradicts the explicit ask.

- **Fix:** Either re-add '15m' to DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS and thread it through Adzuna max_days_old / JSearch date_posted to honor REQ-12022, or record the deliberate drop (mark REQ-12022's 15m portion abandoned) so the gap is intentional and traceable.

##### `p2-m-i18n-004` · p2-m-i18n · _verification_

**i18n:extract injects unresolved template-literal artifacts ('${base}.details', 'jobs.trust.flags.${flag}', 'jobs.boards.<id>') into both locales, so its output is unsafe to commit despite the natural 'run extract to sync locales' expectation.**

The static extractor cannot resolve dynamic t() keys and materializes the literal template text as garbage keys. Sources: PerformancePreferences uses t(`${base}.label`) (line 66) -> extractor emits a '${base}' namespace; trust-badge.tsx uses t(`jobs.trust.flags.${flag}`) (line 46) -> emits 'jobs.trust.flags.${flag}'; jobs.boards.${id} call sites (e.g. ScrapeForm/AutopilotCard) -> emits 'jobs.boards.<id>'. i18next.config.ts (line 9) acknowledges these dynamic patterns (models.tier.${tier}, analyze.groups.${id}) as justification for removeUnusedKeys:false, but nothing suppresses the false ADDs. CI restores them (Finding 002), yet a developer running `pnpm --filter @ajh/translations i18n:extract` locally and committing would inject 3 junk keys into en+de. Not in committed files today (they are on the extract/+ side only), so no user impact — a latent hygiene trap.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/components/preferences/PerformancePreferences/index.tsx:66` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/trust-badge.tsx:46` @ `88e9ab0f01` · `packages/translations/i18next.config.ts:9` @ `977cea621f`
- **Requirements:** REQ-06050, REQ-09020
- **Fix:** Never commit raw i18n:extract output; strip the dynamic-artifact keys (${base}, jobs.trust.flags.${flag}, jobs.boards.<id>). Optionally add an ignore/pattern list in i18next.config.ts so these are not materialized.

##### `p2-m-js-002` · p2-m-js · _verification_

**The Storybook vitest setup calls setProjectAnnotations, which the installed Storybook (10.4.6) reports as redundant and removable, so the setup file is stale config.**

verify/js-tests.log (lines 2-9) opens with a Storybook Info box: 'Found a setup file with setProjectAnnotations. Skipping automatic provisioning of preview annotations to avoid conflicts. Since Storybook 10.3, @storybook/addon-vitest applies these automatically. You can safely remove the setProjectAnnotations call from your setup file, or remove the file entirely if you don't have custom code there.' The only setProjectAnnotations call in-tree is packages/ui/.storybook/vitest.setup.ts line 10 (import at line 2). The tooling itself flags the call as dead/no-op under the current Storybook version — doc/config drift, harmless but noise on every test run.

- **Evidence:** `packages/ui/.storybook/vitest.setup.ts:10` @ `88e9ab0f01`
- **Requirements:** REQ-02054
- **Fix:** Remove the redundant setProjectAnnotations([previewAnnotations]) call (and, if vitest.setup.ts holds no other custom setup, delete the file) per the Storybook 10.3+ addon-vitest auto-provisioning notice, silencing the per-run info banner.

##### `p2-m-js-003` · p2-m-js · _verification_

**turbo declares test.outputs=["coverage/**"] but @ajh/extension's test script runs `vitest run` with no coverage, so turbo warns 'no output files found' and cannot cache extension test results.**

verify/extension.log line 72: 'WARNING no output files found for task @ajh/extension#test. Please check your `outputs` key in turbo.json'. Root turbo.json line 18 sets the shared `test` task outputs to ["coverage/**"], but apps/extension/package.json's `test` script is plain `vitest run` (no --coverage) and there is no apps/extension/turbo.json override, so the extension produces no coverage/** artifact. The workspace coverage harness (REQ-02054, 80/80/80 thresholds) therefore does not receive extension coverage, and turbo's cache for this task is effectively defeated (empty outputs). Hygiene / cache-correctness only.

- **Evidence:** `turbo.json:18` @ `9b0078a6f4`
- **Requirements:** REQ-02054
- **Fix:** Either give @ajh/extension a per-package turbo override with `test.outputs: []`, or add `--coverage` to its test script so it emits coverage/** and folds into the workspace coverage harness; either removes the warning and restores correct turbo caching.

##### `renderer-feat-2-001` · renderer-feat-2 · _orphans_

**A complete command-palette shortcut IPC seam survives across all four layers even though the command palette was fully removed and permanently declared out-of-scope, and nothing emits or consumes it.**

REQ-01068 (PR #112, a7e6cb5c) removed the command palette 'entirely (component, app-store store, titlebar trigger)', and the owner reaffirmed it as permanently out of scope (REQ-16273 'Never propose a command palette', REQ-04037 'explicitly do NOT build a command palette'). The keyboard-shortcuts implementation that replaced it (use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts) even documents 'Intentionally NOT a command palette (out of scope)'. Yet the full `shortcut:command-palette` seam remains wired end-to-end: Rust const SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE (events.rs:20) which is DEFINED BUT NEVER EMITTED anywhere in src-tauri; shared SHORTCUTS_EVENTS.commandPalette + ShortcutsContract.onCommandPalette (whose doc still claims 'the renderer subscribes'); tauri-client shortcuts.onCommandPalette; and the exported renderer hook useCommandPaletteShortcut (use-support.ts:76) which has ZERO consumers anywhere in apps/. - **Requirements:** REQ-01068, REQ-16273, REQ-04037
- **Fix:** Delete the dead command-palette seam: remove useCommandPaletteShortcut (use-support.ts), shortcuts.onCommandPalette (tauri-client), ShortcutsContract/SHORTCUTS_EVENTS.commandPalette (shared), and SHORTCUT_COMMAND_PALETTE (Rust events.rs) plus the events.test entry — nothing emits or consumes it, and it contradicts the standing 'never build a command palette' directive.

##### `renderer-feat-2-002` · renderer-feat-2 · _drift_

**The /resumes -> /documents route rename was left half-done: the route constant, the g-jump shortcut key, the session slice, and the i18n namespace are all still named for the retired 'resumes' concept.**

REQ-05022 (0dc22288) reframed the Resumes page as Documents (3 tabs) while keeping the /resumes route; the route was later renamed to /documents (routes/documents.tsx exists, no resumes.tsx; the notification-route allowlist lists '/documents'). But the identifier rename never completed: ROUTES.RESUMES still resolves to '/documents' (routes.ts:8), the keyboard 'g r' jump is keyed off ROUTES.RESUMES (use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts:14), the Sidebar entry uses ROUTES.RESUMES with label nav.documents, and the DocumentsPage still drives off the session store `resumes` slice and the `resumes.*` i18n namespace. Behavior is correct (everything resolves to /documents), but the stale 'resumes' naming is a ghost that misleads readers about where the Documents route lives.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/constants/routes/routes.ts:8` @ `0dc222887a` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts:14` @ `0dc222887a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-05022, REQ-16240
- **Fix:** Rename ROUTES.RESUMES -> ROUTES.DOCUMENTS (and the GO_TO/Sidebar references) to match the /documents route; optionally rename the `resumes` session slice and i18n namespace in a follow-up so the Documents feature reads consistently.

##### `renderer-feat-2-003` · renderer-feat-2 · _traceability_

**Jobs-list scroll restore on return from an application was wired only into the default split view; the still-selectable list view resets to the top.**

REQ-14010 asked to 'preserve list scroll position when returning from an application (jobs page ...)'; it shipped in 8dee5d67 (PR #506), whose message says 'Restore the jobs list scroll position when returning from an application'. That commit added the persist/restore logic only to JobsSplitView/index.tsx (+32 lines) and never touched JobsResults/index.tsx. JobsResults' list-mode virtualizer uses a fresh scrollRef (line 87) with no save/restore against the session store's jobs.listScrollTop, and viewMode ('list' | 'split') is itself a persisted, user-selectable toggle in the JobsPage header. So a user in list view who opens a job (navigating to /applications/$id) and returns lands back at the top of the list — the exact regression the ask set out to fix, unaddressed for the non-default view.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/jobs/components/JobsResults/index.tsx:87` @ `8dee5d672a` · `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/jobs/components/JobsSplitView/index.tsx:67` @ `8dee5d672a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-14010
- **Fix:** Either mirror JobsSplitView's rAF-throttled scrollTop persist/restore in JobsResults' list-mode scroll container (reusing jobs.listScrollTop), or accept list mode as out of scope and note it — but the current asymmetry silently drops the ask for the list view.

##### `renderer-feat-3-003` · renderer-feat-3 · _orphans_

**Settings search still advertises login boards (indeed, glassdoor, xing, stepstone) that the Accounts tab no longer offers — AUTH_BOARDS is now LinkedIn-only.**

The settings search index (added by REQ-12024, PR #470) gives the 'accounts-boards' entry keywords ['...','linkedin','indeed','glassdoor','xing','stepstone','connect','sign in']. But the multi-board login list was collapsed: constants/auth/auth.ts now defines AUTH_BOARDS as LinkedIn only (AuthBoardId = 'linkedin'), and AccountsSettingsTab maps AUTH_BOARDS -> BoardSessionRow, so only LinkedIn renders. REQ-16630's own deferral note flags this drift ('glassdoor still shows'), though glassdoor is now gone from AUTH_BOARDS and lingers only in these stale search synonyms. Searching 'glassdoor'/'xing'/'stepstone' therefore jumps the user to a boards section that only offers LinkedIn login.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src/renderer/features/settings/lib/search-index.ts:274` @ `ca59779f66`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16630, REQ-05017
- **Fix:** Trim the 'accounts-boards' search keywords to boards actually present in AUTH_BOARDS (LinkedIn), or, better, derive the keyword list from AUTH_BOARDS so it can never drift again.

##### `rust-ai-b-002` · rust-ai-b · _orphans_

**AiGenerationStore::applied_job_urls is an orphaned public method: the 'applied' derivation it was built for was moved to ApplicationStore::applied_job_urls (ADR 0001) and no production caller remains.**

REQ-04014 (PR #210/#211) introduced deriving a found job's 'applied' badge from an ai_generations job_url link, implemented via AiGenerationStore::applied_job_urls. Commit 3e85c341 (PR #359, application tracking / ADR 0001) switched commands::autopilot::enrich_applied from AiGenerationStore to ApplicationStore::applied_job_urls (status past 'saved'), but left the old method in place. Today its only reference outside its own module tests is nothing — a grep for applied_job_urls shows the autopilot command using the ApplicationStore version exclusively. Being pub, it also produces no dead-code warning, so it will not rot visibly. The two methods additionally have subtly different semantics (any saved generation vs. status ≠ saved, normalized vs. raw URL), so a future caller reaching for the wrong one would silently reintroduce the pre-ADR-0001 behavior.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/ai_generations/mod.rs:325` @ `3e85c34180` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/autopilot.rs:33` @ `3e85c34180`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04014, REQ-16245
- **Fix:** Delete AiGenerationStore::applied_job_urls and its test, or mark it #[deprecated]/#[cfg(test)] with a pointer to ApplicationStore::applied_job_urls as the single source of the applied derivation.

##### `rust-ai-b-004` · rust-ai-b · _consistency_

**run_agent's doc comment still describes the Phase-1 behavior ('Write tools are DENIED — Phase-1 human-in-the-loop guard') while the same function implements the Phase-3 confirm-gate suspend, contradicting the code three screens below it.**

Phase 3 (REQ-15029, commit ce0558f3 / PR #556) replaced the Phase-1 auto-deny of Write tools with the suspend-for-confirmation gate; the loop body (controller.rs ToolKind::Write arm, line 440+) and the AgentStep.denied field doc were updated accordingly, but the function-level doc of run_agent still asserts writes are denied outright. A reader (or a future refactor validating against the doc) gets the opposite safety model from the one implemented and security-reviewed: deny-by-default vs suspend-and-execute-on-approval.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/agent/controller.rs:240` @ `ce0558f31c`
- **Requirements:** REQ-15029, REQ-16235
- **Fix:** Update run_agent's doc paragraph to say Write tool calls SUSPEND on the confirm gate (resolve_write) and only execute after an explicit approve decision, matching the ToolKind::Write arm and the module-level SECURITY invariant.

##### `rust-data-003` · rust-data · _consistency_

REQ-06048 (PRs #323/#328, commit cc14afdb) removed the chat feature and its conversations SQLite store, adding a best-effort boot-time deletion of the orphaned conversations.db as GDPR cleanup (lib.rs:659-670). docs/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md:398-408 (the REQ-03083 decision) still lists conversations.db 'Chat history' among the current databases and reasons about its corruption isolation, while omitting the later applications.db (PR #359) and referrals.db (PR #265) stores that do exist. The doc describes a store set that matches neither the pre-removal nor the current state.

##### `rust-data-004` · rust-data · _consistency_

REQ-03052 recorded ADR-007 (ai_generations as the application aggregate, applied derived from generation rows). REQ-08057 (PR #381, commit 74199796) removed '13 dead IPC commands' as part of the whole-codebase health sweep. privacy_clear_data (commands/privacy.rs:173-184, a hybrid of privacy_sign_out_all + postings/interactions clear) is still registered in lib.rs:840 but is invocable by nothing: the tauri-client privacy namespace exposes only signOutAll/clearInteractions/resetApp, packages/shared has no clearData contract, and no renderer code references it. It also duplicates the hardcoded 4-board disconnect list a third time.

##### `rust-docs-embed-002` · rust-docs-embed · _orphans_

**PostingsCache's in-memory `embeddings` cache is production-dead: `set_embedding`/`get_embedding` are called only from tests; nothing in the app ever populates or reads it, so its doc comment ('populated lazily by hybrid search') describes a Rust hybrid search that does not exist.**

REQ-02038 shipped hybrid job search over the live PostingsCache with a lazy per-posting embedding cache, but that was a TypeScript feature; the Rust port never landed it (REQ-16223 records 'hybrid search currently a stub in Rust'). The live match path embeds job text through the SQLite `posting_vectors` table via `posting_vector_or_embed`, not this in-memory map. Grep confirms `set_embedding`/`get_embedding` have no non-test callers, so the `embeddings: HashMap` field is always empty in production, and the invalidation branches that `remove` from it in `PostingsCache::add` and `update_description` (the REQ-16092/REQ-16423 staleness guard) operate on an always-empty map — the real staleness protection is the `text_hash` key in `posting_vectors`. `clear_embeddings` (called from `ai_reembed_all`) clears an empty map. This is leftover machinery from the abandoned Rust hybrid-search direction.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/postings/mod.rs:26` @ `81f6c70cc8` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/postings/mod.rs:125` @ `81f6c70cc8`
- **Requirements:** REQ-02038, REQ-16223, REQ-16092, REQ-16423
- **Fix:** Delete the `embeddings` field, `get_embedding`/`set_embedding`/`clear_embeddings`, the two `embeddings.remove` invalidation branches, their tests, and the `ai_reembed_all` `clear_embeddings` call — or implement the Rust hybrid search that was supposed to populate it. Update the doc comments that claim it is 'populated lazily by hybrid search.'

##### `rust-docs-embed-003` · rust-docs-embed · _consistency_

**The server maps an omitted `semanticScoringEnabled` flag to semantic scoring ENABLED (`semantic_enabled_bit(None) → 1`), contradicting the documented 'matching defaults to keyword-only; semantic is opt-in' contract.**

REQ-16714 (API.md) and REQ-16011/ADR-017:107 state the default scoring path is keyword-only and semantic is opt-in via a caller-supplied flag. But `semantic_enabled_bit` treats only `Some(false)` as off; `None` (flag omitted) is treated as ON. The shipped renderer masks this because it always sends the flag explicitly (preferences default `semanticScoring: false` → use-match.ts sends `semanticScoringEnabled: false`). The latent divergence bites any caller that omits the flag: the agent `match_resume` tool schema marks `semanticScoringEnabled` optional (agent/tools.rs), so an omitting invocation with an embedding provider configured runs semantic scoring + embed cost — the opposite of the documented opt-in default. (When no provider is available it degrades to keyword-only, which hides the effect.)

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/match_resume.rs:40` @ `81f6c70cc8`
- **Requirements:** REQ-16714, REQ-16011
- **Fix:** Make the safe default match the documented contract: map `None` to `0` (keyword-only) in `semantic_enabled_bit`, so semantic scoring is truly opt-in for every caller, not just the renderer that happens to pass the flag.

##### `rust-docs-embed-004` · rust-docs-embed · _drift_

**Multiple developer-facing docs still describe the documents/embeddings pipeline as pdfjs/mammoth extraction + a LanceDB vector store (`vectors/`), a subsystem deleted when extraction moved to Rust (pdf-extract/quick-xml) and vectors moved into SQLite (documents.db).**

REQ-01009 (PR #69) moved the document store, extraction, embeddings, and vector search into native Rust with SQLite JSON-array vectors + pure-Rust cosine, deleting the Node data-store; REQ-02012 (PR #120) replaced pdfjs/mammoth with per-format Rust extractors. No LanceDB dependency or `vectors/` directory exists in the app today (vectors live in the `vectors` TABLE inside documents.db). Yet DEVELOPMENT.md still tells devs the 'LanceDB vector store is in the same directory under vectors/' and to reset by deleting 'app.db and vectors/'; DEPLOYMENT.md diagrams a `vectors/ ← LanceDB vector store`; PATTERNS.md diagrams 'pdfjs extract', 'mammoth extract', 'Upsert to LanceDB', and 'ANN search in LanceDB'; ARCHITECTURE.md documents 'SQLite + LanceDB' and a 'LanceDB Collections' section. These instructions are actively misleading (a dev would look for a nonexistent `vectors/` dir) and the search/extraction descriptions contradict the shipped SQLite/pdf-extract implementation.

- **Evidence:** `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md:245` @ `f50b99a6ac` · `docs/DEPLOYMENT.md:327` @ `f50b99a6ac` · `docs/PATTERNS.md:284` @ `5e876ed41a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-01009, REQ-02012, REQ-16490, REQ-12040, REQ-12041, REQ-16223
- **Fix:** Update ARCHITECTURE.md / PATTERNS.md / DEPLOYMENT.md / DEVELOPMENT.md to describe the current pipeline (Rust pdf-extract + DOCX zip/quick-xml + Tesseract.js OCR fallback; embeddings + vectors stored in the `vectors` table of documents.db; pure-Rust cosine; no LanceDB, no `vectors/` dir; reset = delete the *.db files).

##### `rust-platform-002` · rust-platform · _consistency_

**AppError::Cancelled is annotated #[allow(dead_code)] and documented as reserved/unused, but it is actively returned by the scraping fetch_text helper.**

In the centralized error module the Cancelled variant carries `#[allow(dead_code)]` and the comment 'Reserved for the cancellation paths that currently surface bespoke messages' (implying it is not yet constructed), with the preceding doc sentence duplicated. But scraping/http/mod.rs::fetch_text returns `AppError::Cancelled` on a cancelled token in a reachable production path (line 109 pre-send check, and again at line 254 in the transport-error branch), added with the fetch_text streaming rework in PR #454/REQ-11036; the variant is also matched in AppError::code(). It is therefore genuinely constructed and live, so the dead_code allow is superfluous (dead_code would not fire) and the 'reserved/unused' comment is stale and misleading. Harmless at runtime, but a code-vs-comment incoherence in the one module that is supposed to be the authoritative typed-error surface.

- **Evidence:** `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/http/mod.rs:109` @ `9b40e7bdbd` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/error.rs:40` @ `04e6e78aed`
- **Requirements:** REQ-11036
- **Fix:** Remove the now-superfluous #[allow(dead_code)] on AppError::Cancelled and correct the comment to note it is returned by fetch_text (and de-duplicate the doubled doc sentence).

##### `scraping-a-002` · scraping-a · _drift_

**docs/API.md still advertises '21 active scrapers' with a 21-id list that omits workable and comeet, but the SCRAPERS registry now contains 23 boards.**

PR #535 (commit 47111d9a, subject 'scraping hardening + workable and comeet boards (21 to 23)') added WorkableScraper and ComeetScraper to scraping/boards/mod.rs SCRAPERS, taking the registry to 23. docs/API.md:406 still reads 'Supported board IDs (21 active scrapers, canonical list: BOARD_IDS ...)' and enumerates exactly 21 ids without workable or comeet. REQ-15001's own note pre-flagged this exact drift ('SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md says 23 active scrapers while API.md says 21') as an open remnant; it is still stale at HEAD. This directly violates the project's stated invariant (REQ-16059 / scraping-applier-expert): board counts must be read from the SCRAPERS registry in source, never a copied doc number.

- **Evidence:** `docs/API.md:406` @ `47111d9a08` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/mod.rs:79` @ `47111d9a08`
- **Requirements:** REQ-15001, REQ-16059, REQ-14041, REQ-14042
- **Fix:** Update docs/API.md:406 to '23 active scrapers' and add workable + comeet to the id list, or (better, per REQ-16059) drop the hardcoded count/list and point at BOARD_IDS / the SCRAPERS registry as the single source of truth.

##### `scraping-a-003` · scraping-a · _traceability_

**The 'past 15 minutes' date-filter option the owner explicitly requested was silently dropped; the finest preset is now 30m.**

REQ-12022 asked to add finer-grained posted-date filters: 'past 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours, 4 hours, and 8 hours'. The centralized constant set established in PR #473 (commit 99e7286d) is DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS = ['30m','1h','2h','4h','8h','24h','week','month'] (packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts:135) — the 15-minute option is absent, and the LinkedIn direct scraper's f_TPR map likewise has no 15m arm (finest is Some("30m") -> r1800 in linkedin/api_client/mod.rs:131). So the ask is only partially delivered. This may have been an intentional consolidation (the aggregator collapses every sub-day window to whole-day granularity), but no ledger/ADR entry records dropping 15m specifically, so it reads as a silently-dropped ask.

- **Evidence:** `packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts:135` @ `99e7286d3a`
- **Requirements:** REQ-12022
- **Fix:** Either add '15m' back to DATE_FILTER_OPTIONS and the LinkedIn f_TPR map (r900) to honor the original ask, or record an explicit note (ADR/lesson) that 15m was intentionally dropped because the aggregator backends only support whole-day recency.

##### `scraping-b-004` · scraping-b · _consistency_

**Three docs state three different board counts (20, 21, 23) against the 23-entry SCRAPERS registry, violating the standing rule that board counts must come from the registry — a drift already flagged as an open remnant at PR #535 and never fixed.**

At HEAD: docs/API.md:406 claims '21 active scrapers' and its 'canonical' id list omits workable and comeet; docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70 says 'Active scrapers: 21 boards'; docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:439 says 'Job boards (20)' and attributes scraping to 'chromiumoxide (browser) / net::http' although no registered scraper returns ScraperMode::Browser anymore (chromiumoxide remains only for board_login/LinkedIn connect). REQ-16059 records the rule 'board counts must always come from the SCRAPERS registry — never trust a copied number in docs', REQ-14033 assigns count sync to the steward's doc pass, and REQ-15001's own note flagged the 21-vs-23 mismatch as an open remnant — still open.

- **Evidence:** `docs/API.md:406` @ `47111d9a08` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:70` @ `47111d9a08` · `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:439` @ `47111d9a08` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/boards/mod.rs:57` @ `47111d9a08`
- **Requirements:** REQ-15001, REQ-16059, REQ-14033
- **Fix:** One steward doc-sync pass: set all three docs to the registry-derived count (23), add workable/comeet to the API.md id list, and reword the ARCHITECTURE.md dependency row to 'net::http (all boards); chromiumoxide (board login only)'.

##### `testing-002` · testing · _drift_

**docs/architecture-rules.md — billed as the 'formal, testable contract ... duplicated verbatim' in tests/architecture.rs — has drifted from the enforced test: it still lists the removed `conversations` and `browser` modules and stale R3/R4 allowlist entries the test correctly dropped.**

The doc (Last updated 2026-06-01) asserts (line 31-33) that its layer list is 'the single source of truth and is duplicated verbatim as the LAYER table' in the test. It is not. (a) `conversations` was removed in #328 (cc14afd, 2026-06-09, 'remove the dead conversations chat backend') yet still appears in the doc's L1 list (line 24), R2 allowlist (line 130), and R3 owners (line 131); the enforced test omits it in all three (0 matches). (b) `browser` (doc L0 list line 29, L0 contract line 39) existed at cc14afd but is absent by the rename commit 04e6e78; the test's L0 array omits it. (c) The R4 row (line 132) still claims a `commands/ai_provider/{cli_agent,ollama}` env-allowlist exception, but env access is fully centralized — no `std::env::var`/`AJH_DATA_DIR` exists outside platform/**, and the enforced test's R4_ALLOW is empty (architecture.rs line 352, comment 'no non-platform source needs an allowlist entry'). (d) The doc's R3 '9 genuine owners' (line 131) omits the newer `applications`, `contact_profile`, and `scraping/board_login/import.rs` rusqlite sites that the enforced R3_ALLOW correctly lists (verified: those files use rusqlite::). No behavioral risk — the enforced test is the correct/current artifact — but the doc-vs-test parity the owner asserted (REQ-04003/REQ-12053) is unmet and nothing guards it.

- **Evidence:** `docs/architecture-rules.md:24` @ `cc14afdb39` · `docs/architecture-rules.md:131` @ `cc14afdb39` · `docs/architecture-rules.md:29` @ `04e6e78aed` · `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/architecture.rs:352` @ `a6face0339`
- **Requirements:** REQ-04003, REQ-12053
- **Fix:** Update docs/architecture-rules.md to match the enforced test: drop `conversations` (lines 24, 130, 131) and `browser` (lines 29, 39) from the layer lists/contracts, remove the stale R4 `commands/ai_provider` env exception (line 132), and refresh the R3 owner list to add applications/contact_profile/board_login. Consider a small CI/doc-sync check (or codegen) that fails when the doc's layer table + allowlists diverge from the test constants, since the owner requires them to stay 'verbatim'.

##### `ui-package-004` · ui-package · _traceability_

**REQ-08011's requested Tiptap extension suite for the RichTextEditor (underline, color, font size, line height, text align, bubble menu, drag handle) never landed and was silently superseded by the locked export-safe schema, without the ask ever being reconciled.**

The 2026-06-12 ask (REQ-08011, PR #371, commit 2655d99) included 'a list of Tiptap extensions (color, font size, line height, underline, bubble menu, drag handle, text align, lists, etc.)'; the ledger still carries it as status=partial with BubbleMenu/DragHandle 'pending'. Current buildEditorExtensions() (extensions.ts) deliberately DISABLES underline (line 77) along with strike/code/etc., and contains no bubble-menu, drag-handle, color, font-size, line-height, or text-align extensions — the later locked-schema contract (REQ-13024, DESIGN_SYSTEM.md 'constrained to the vocabulary that survives export') won. Additionally the in-session decision 'drop italic' recorded in REQ-08011 is contradicted by italic remaining in the enabled vocabulary (extensions.ts line 55 comment and the DESIGN_SYSTEM.md RichTextEditor contract), so that sub-decision was also reversed without a record. No user-facing defect — the locked schema is the deliberate, documented direction — but the ledger entry is stale and the original ask was dropped silently rather than superseded.

- **Evidence:** `packages/ui/src/components/RichTextEditor/extensions.ts:77` @ `2655d99d12` · `packages/ui/src/components/RichTextEditor/extensions.ts:55` @ `2655d99d12`
- **Requirements:** REQ-08011, REQ-13024
- **Fix:** Mark the REQ-08011 extension-suite sub-ask as superseded by the locked export-safe schema (REQ-13024/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md RichTextEditor contract) so the 'pending BubbleMenu/DragHandle' state stops reading as unfinished work.

### 6. Open Questions

These resolve genuine ambiguities in the owner's _original intent_ — surfaced by lifetime requirement conflicts (§5), by findings the arbiters downgraded because severity hinged on whether dead code is deliberate, and by unclear ledger asks with no traceable outcome; each should be answered before any triage/fix work, because the answer changes what the code should do (or confirms a deliberate shelving).

**Intent conflicts** — code currently contradicts a later or documented ask; the owner must pick a direction.

1. **Does the opt-in Clearbit company-name lookup violate the local-first / only-outbound privacy guarantee, or should the guarantee be amended to disclose it?** Conflict REQ-16455 ⇄ REQ-16108: the shipped opt-in Clearbit egress (PR #499) contradicts both privacy promises, yet the docs still claim only AI-provider + web-search traffic ever leaves the machine. Unblocks the choice to remove/re-gate the egress versus rewrite the privacy statement and consent copy — a user-facing privacy claim, so the highest-priority decision here.

2. **Should semantic (hybrid) match scoring default ON or OFF, and is the removed Cmd+K hybrid-search surface gone for good?** Conflicts REQ-02038 ⇄ REQ-16011 (renderer + autopilot default keyword-only while Rust `semantic_enabled_bit(None)` and the agent tool default semantic-ON) and REQ-16239 ⇄ REQ-02038 (hybrid-search UI removed, dead plumbing + a stale comment left behind). Unblocks which side to flip so one default wins, and whether to delete the orphaned hybrid-search plumbing.

3. **Is on-demand per-job match scoring the final design, making `match_resume_batch` a ghost to delete?** Four pairs — REQ-08040 / REQ-16011 / REQ-16714 / REQ-06058, each ⇄ REQ-13028 — show the ledger recording batch-all-postings as the implemented mechanism while the live path scores per-job on open; the batch command stays registered and client-wired with no caller. Unblocks deleting the batch command/tests/docs versus re-wiring a caller for it.

4. **When the extension auth handshake times out, should it reconnect (current behavior), report `bad_token`, or raise a distinct `auth_timeout` phase?** Conflict REQ-11004 ⇄ REQ-16627: the timeout-phase decision was never recorded, the code treats an auth timeout as a transport failure, and the ledger note misstates the shipped behavior. Unblocks the handshake state machine and its security review.

5. **Is the project staying on 0.x semver (breaking change → minor), or moving to breaking → major?** a-wins conflict REQ-01034 ⇄ REQ-03080: the release code keeps the 0.x guard (breaking→minor) while later docs assert BREAKING→major. Unblocks the semantic-release / commitlint configuration and the release docs.

6. **Confirm `/documents` is the canonical route and the "keep `/resumes`" clause is superseded?** a-wins conflict REQ-09023 ⇄ REQ-16240: the route file is `createFileRoute('/documents')`, but REQ-16240's canonical clause still says keep `/resumes`, unmarked. Unblocks marking the stale clause superseded (doc-only) or, if `/resumes` was truly intended, adding a redirect.

**Dropped or deferred** — features shipped-but-unwired or documented-but-unimplemented; build it or formally drop it?

7. **Is `structuredOutputFor` a real planned capability, or abandoned scaffolding to delete?** Unresolved conflict REQ-03028 ⇄ REQ-08048: it shipped and is documented but has never been consumed, and the deferred wiring is still absent. Unblocks wiring a consumer versus removing the code and its doc claim.

8. **Commit to PDF/UA tagged-PDF accessibility, or drop the claim?** Unresolved conflict REQ-03044 ⇄ REQ-04031: the tagged-output accessibility standard is documented, but the Typst engine exports with `PdfOptions::default()` — never implemented and never formally deferred. Unblocks implementing tagged export versus removing the accessibility promise from the export standard/docs.

9. **Are the splash `app_ready` / theme-mirror follow-ups moot now that the native splash was deleted?** REQ-16637 and REQ-16638 (with the native-splash-superseded pairs REQ-16635 / REQ-16636 ⇄ REQ-12065): the native splash + `app_ready` IPC + theme-mirror were removed (#475) and replaced by the React AppSplash, yet the deferred "add `app_ready` to SystemContract" and "add the splash module to the L3 doc list" tasks are still open. Unblocks closing them as obsolete versus re-opening them against the React splash.

10. **Which of the explicitly-deferred UX / roadmap items are still planned versus quietly abandoned?** A cluster of "optional future"/deferred follow-ups with no implementation evidence: the scraped-salary badge (REQ-16659), the Jobs LinkedIn-style redesign (REQ-16642 — decisions locked on a branch but every status item left unchecked, so even shipped-vs-not is unknown), catalog-derived `AUTH_BOARDS` (REQ-16630), Adzuna.de German-market depth monitoring (REQ-16681), and the empty-release-notes guard (REQ-16623). Unblocks roadmap triage — which to schedule, which to close.

**Ambiguous status** — dead/unreachable code or untraceable asks; deliberate or accidental?

11. **Is the Support page intentionally FAQ-only, or were the ContactFeedback and RecoveryTools panels accidentally disconnected?** Downgraded findings p2-b1-ipc-chain-001 / -002 and (relatedly) the credentials probe, tied to REQ-00040 and REQ-11007: both panels are orphaned dead code invoking Rust commands that were never registered — the recovery panel even shows false "success" toasts for destructive resets that never run — and the arbiter downgraded them _only_ because they are currently unrendered. Unblocks the sharpest either/or in the audit: delete the dead panels + their contracts, or implement/register the missing commands and fix the false-success handler.

12. **Should a factory / privacy reset wipe the Chromium board-login profiles under `browser-state/<board>/`?** Downgraded finding rust-data-001 (REQ-04008, REQ-16469): reset disconnects boards but never deletes the profile directories, so an authenticated session can silently resume — and the disconnect code comment says nuking the profile dir was _deliberately_ avoided as "less reliable." Unblocks whether to add best-effort profile deletion (a privacy fix) or to document the retained-profile behavior as intended.

13. **Is OS-encryption availability meant to be really detected, or permanently assumed available?** Downgraded finding ipc-a-001 (REQ-05017, REQ-16041): `is_available()` is hardcoded `true`, so the "no OS encryption" warning is unreachable and masks a real type drift between the Rust return (`{available:bool}`) and the TS contract (bare `bool`). Unblocks implementing real detection + fixing the drift, versus deleting the warning path as dead.

14. **Did the analysis-only audits and untraceable diagnostic asks leave any still-open action items, and should the truncated prior fleet-audit tails be re-run?** Merges the unclear analysis/verification asks that recorded no outcome — cache/storage efficiency (REQ-08038), import/export end-to-end (REQ-13006), MCP-server liveness (REQ-13037, REQ-15008), the `/security-review` gate (REQ-14005) — with the prior fleet-audit artifacts that lost their tails to truncation and still cite the pre-rename `apps/tauri/**` paths (REQ-10036, REQ-10037). Unblocks whether hidden findings remain unaddressed or these can all be closed as no-ops.
